C: 隔着无线电，他亲自见证了无人救援的爱人，是如何在破损的航器中耗尽了氧气，又是如何在无尽的黑暗漂流中缓慢走向死亡。
E: Through the radio, he personally witnessed how his stranded lover ran out of oxygen in the damaged aircraft and how she slowly died drifting into the endless darkness.

C: 好啦，故事今天就讲到这里。那么这些报酬请拿着，希望以后还有机会见到你。

C: 「模因病毒」？

C: 我想说…对…对不起呜呜呜。

C: 不必急着为自己辩护，因为真相的揭露不是现在！

C: 不论他们表现得多么正气凛然，多么威逼利诱，多么可怜兮兮，也不能说漏嘴！
E: No matter how righteous they appear, how charismatic they are, how pitiable they present themselves, we must not tell them anything!

C: 嗯，因为我刚刚说漏了嘴，阿德勒在帮我打圆场呢。
E: That's right. I messed up just now, so Adler is now helping me not tell on myself any more than I already did.

C: 难以置信，空间站怎么会有这玩意儿？科员们就算再有想法，也不至于敢在墙上乱涂乱画，更何况还是…一滴汗？
E: Unbelievable. Why would something like this be in the space station? The staff wouldn't even dare scribble on the wall, let alone leave... a drop of sweat?

C: 给，把镜头对准墙上的涂鸦就行了。
E: Here — just point it at the graffiti on the wall.

C: 你猜后来发生了什么？她反向骇入我的设备，给一堆人发了垃圾短信，整了个面向全空间站的游戏，告诉所有人有个叫伦纳德的呆瓜闯了大祸……
E: Can you guess what happened next? She reverse-hacked my device, sent out a bunch of spam, made a video game open to the space station, and told everyone that an idiot named Leonard made a big, silly mistake...

C: 是不想管吧。
E: Normally she doesn't want to deal with it.

C: 之前没把这两件事联系在一起，但如果那位骇客在完成星核猎手的任务之外，还怀着自己的个人目的——
E: I wasn't able to put the two together before. If the hacker has a personal objective other than the Stellaron Hunter's mission...

C: 我明白了！我明白了！星核猎手的行动，不过是她的障眼法！
E: I got it! I got it now! The Stellaron Hunters' operation was just a cover for her!

C: 好极了，岂止有访问记录，整个终端传输都被她关闭了——我之前还以为是军团造成的。
E: Just perfect. Not only did she shut off all the access logs, she shut off all the terminal transmissions too... I thought it was the Legion that did this.

C: 你不想干，就退出吧，自己去跟剩下两个人讲…哦，把你的技术也一起带走，我不稀罕。
E: Leave if you don't want to be a part of this. Go tell the other two yourself... Oh, and take your tech with you. I don't need it.

C: 是的。除了刚才提到的原因，还有一些额外考量。
E: Yes. There are other concerns besides those I've mentioned.

C: 你看看办公室牌子上写的是谁的名字，我为什么不可以回来？哼，要走也不是我走，我偏要站这儿。
E: Do you not see whose name it is on the desk? Hmph, I can come and go as I wish. Besides, if anyone is going to leave, it definitely won't be me.

C: 这是正常现象，大多数有机生命在经历信标投射后都会产生不适，你看见了什么？
E: That is a normal reaction. Most organic lifeforms will experience discomfort after undergoing beacon projection. What do you see?

C: 有位健谈的同行者也不失为一种乐趣，不是吗？
E: It's a nice thing to have a companion who likes to talk, isn't it?

C: 是的，这仍是一段影像记录，千真万确。
E: Yes. This is a visual record. No doubt about it.

C: 没错。以太卡带的数据就在下一个房间，我们的追踪也快要结束了。
E: That's correct. The Aether Cartridge data is in the next room. Our pursuit is coming to an end.

C: 跟我来！
E: Come with me!

C: 还在准备安全演练吗？
E: Are you still preparing for the drill?

C: 他接着纠正我：「是伊德莉拉，不是德伊莉拉。」
E: Then, he corrected me, "It's Idrila, not Adrila."

C: 行吧，反正我也没别处可去，就先跟你——
E: *sigh* Alright, I have nowhere else to be. Let's go.

C: 我与你们同去。维利特，你也来。
E: I'll accompany you. Velite, you too.

C: 你忍不住想抚摸它的凹陷——从没有人在乎它平日承受了怎样的重量，从没有人。
E: You feel the urge to trace your finger along the edge of the seats' depression. No one ever appreciates the weight these seats have to uphold.

C: 车厢的列车座椅底下。
E: Under the car seats.

C: 看上去不像。
E: It doesn't seem like it.

C: 这是…发生什么了？
E: What happened?

C: 有洗车星应该不用我们动手了吧…
E: Phew, so the Washtopia can take care of the spring cleaning...

C: 见着它的痕迹，就给我发个信号。
E: Send me a signal when you see traces of it.

C: 不用啦。好意我心领了，你不是还有黑塔女士的委托要调查么？
E: That's okay, but thank you for the offer. Aren't you helping Madam Herta with something already?

C: （果然，除辛克尔外，我还咨询了另一个人，只记得对方是<color=#dbc291ff>密卷科</color>的。）
E: (So I did consult someone other than Hinkel. The only thing I remember is that they're from the <color=#dbc291ff>Department of Insight</color>.)

C: 你问过界种科的室长吗？
E: Have you asked the Department of Ecology head?

C: （将混淆的记忆复位）
E: (Restore the muddled memory)

C: 为、为什么你会知道？！
E: H—How did you know!?

C: 虽然没有得到直接回答，但艾丝妲为我引荐了合适的科员。
E: I didn't get a direct answer, but Asta recommended I seek out a researcher who could help.

C: 带我去见那个「界种科最了解奇物的人」。
E: Take me to the one who knows the most about Curios within the Department of Ecology, if you would be so kind.

C: 受害者们的下落，暂时还不得而知。但至此，冥火大公的计划已然明了。
E: We don't know where the victims went, but Duke Inferno's plan is now clear.

C: 而下一步，啪——就是点燃火星。看看这些失踪的科员，假设他们的共同点被人发现，会是什么后果？不用我多说。
E: The next step would be to ignite the spark. Look at those researchers who disappeared — what would happen if someone realized their similarities? I need to say no more.

C: 我能见一见阮•梅吗？
E: Would I be able to meet Ruan Mei?

C: 嗯，我也很高兴。我们在模拟宇宙见过。
E: Yes, I'm happy to meet you too. We've met before in the Simulated Universe.

C: …喔，是这事？这你都知道，消息蛮灵通的嘛。这会儿空间站一团乱，有大人物要来，大家原本就够忙的，又不知道哪冒出来一堆小怪物。
E: ...Oh, that? You've got pretty good contacts if you know that already. The space station is a mess. Everyone's already swamped as it is, and now out of nowhere, there are a bunch of little creatures popping up.

C: 我来解决空间站的麻烦！
E: I'll solve the space station's problems!

C: 这件事与他们无关。而且参与的人多了，就会有麻烦。
E: This has nothing to do with them. Besides, the more people involved, the more problems there will be.

C: 阮•梅的表情毫无波澜
E: Ruan Mei is expressionless

C: 没有，你误会了。我只是觉得黑塔开会的这段时间，你能更方便地出入空间站。
E: No. You misunderstand. While Herta is tied up in meetings, you might find it more convenient to roam the space station.

C: 我和母亲在平静的冰川上航行，在世界中寻找生命的痕迹，邂逅各种奇异的现象。
E: My mother and I navigated calm glaciers looking for signs of life on that world... and encountered many bizarre phenomena.

C: {NICKNAME}，在你眼里，我做错了吗？
E: {NICKNAME}, do you believe my actions were wrong?

C: 你知道我在找什么？
E: You know what I'm looking for?

C: 因为我见不得蠢材，当然，他们也不想见我。
E: Because I can't bear to see idiots. Of course, they wouldn't want to see me either.

C: 伟大的科学组合确实需要一张供后人纪念的合照。
E: You're right. A great scientific team will require a photo to serve as a memento for future generations.

C: 我也要补个唇蜜！
E: I want to put on some lip gloss, too!

C: 我…我好怕…不过…你们陪着我的话……
E: I... I'm so scared... But... if you two are with me...

C: 我们死守了七个月，弹药越来越少，库存也都清空了…饿着肚子…多少天了…可我们始终没有放弃阵地。
E: We have held out here for seven months. Our ammo supplies are dwindling, and the storehouse is empty. We've gone hungry for... I don't know how many days now... But we have never abandoned our post.

C: …请问杰帕德长官来了嘛？我跟他挺熟的……
E: ...Is Captain Gepard around? H—He's an old buddy of mine...

C: 虚卒？这个星球被反物质军团入侵过吗？
E: Voidranger? Was this planet invaded by the Antimatter Legion?

C: 所以…所以我很庆幸你们来了——我知道两位原本只是想来参加节日庆典的，但是现在……
E: I'm glad you're both here — I know you came for the festival, but now...

C: 临时估值二十七万……
E: Provisional evaluation would value it at approximately <unbreak>270,000</unbreak>...

C: 我们也没承诺一定成功。
E: We didn't promise Bronya we'd succeed.

C: 哼唧。
E: Oink.

C: 帮、帮帮忙啊！穿黑衣服的打人了！
E: H—Help! Those people dressed in black are attacking us!

C: …本地人？公司正在此处执行资产评估工作，请本地居民跟随负责疏散的员工到安全区域休息……
E: A local? The IPC is currently conducting asset evaluation work here. Local residents are advised to follow those responsible for evacuation to the—

C: 初代大守护者阿丽萨•兰德领导了这项工程。战士们在前线浴血奋战时，工程师们在后方夜以继日地设计、搭建……
E: The project was led by the first Supreme Guardian — Alisa Rand. While warriors fought on the front lines, the engineers worked tirelessly behind the scenes, designing and constructing it...

C: 是，但不完全是。
E: Yes, but not entirely.

C: …「砂金」？你怎么还在？
E: ...Aventurine? Why are you still on the line?

C: 呃…他们好像快吵起来了，咱们还是先走为上吧……
E: Looks like things are getting heated. I think we'd better get outta here.

C: 记住，要尽量保证机兵不被敌人发现——行动越隐蔽，成功率越高。
E: Remember, try to avoid being noticed. Remaining hidden will increase our chances of success.

C: 我该怎么做？
E: What should I do?

C: …不和你扯了，其实我就是没钱继续看病了。虽然娜塔莎医生说我可以一直赊账，等以后有机会再还…但我这人就是脸皮薄。
E: ...What else is there to say. Natasha was seeing me free of charge. She said I could keep a tab running for as long as I liked, then pay it back when I had the opportunity... I guess I'm too proud.

C: 瓦赫留下的那些药物…不对，那些甚至不能算「药物」，只是些实验中产生的副产品、试剂罢了。
E: What Vache left behind is nothing more than reagents and experimental by-products... The word "medicine" doesn't apply here.

C: 各位观众，接下来千万别把你们的目光移开——
E: Dear viewers, no time to blink...

C: <size=36>{NICKNAME}！{NICKNAME}！{NICKNAME}！</size>
E: <size=36>{NICKNAME}! {NICKNAME}! {NICKNAME}!</size>

C: 在全体科员和你这位得力帮手的共同建设之下，「黑塔」已经大致恢复了战前的元气。
E: Thanks to your help and everybody's hard work, the space station is pretty much back on its feet.

C: 至于其他六言句，依我来看，一部分描绘了这位共犯的画像，一部分表述了水魈真正的藏匿地点。可惜，我对如何破解剩下的信息毫无头绪。
E: As for the other six sentences, in my opinion, some depict the portrait of the accomplice and some describe the real hiding place of the Xenohydro. Unfortunately, I have no idea how to interpret the rest of the message.

C: 旁边的收容品说明已被涂黑，显示出脱逃者徒劳的愤怒和有限的反抗。
E: The description of the collectible item nearby has been painted over. This is a sign of anger and frustration exhibited by the escapee.

C: 告密人打从一开始就在劝说我，让我放弃与水魈合谋，独立去过自己的生活？
E: So from the beginning, the informant tried to persuade me to give up my conspiracy with the Xenohydro and live my own life independently...

C: 你的意思是，你不会告诉别人共犯究竟是谁？…谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。
E: You mean, you won't tell them who the accomplice was? Thank you, {NICKNAME}.

C: 行、行啊，可我怕我一开口，这眼泪就止不住……
E: Okay... Okay, but I'm afraid I won't be able to stop my tears as soon as I start to speak...

C: 束手就擒吧，入侵者！我会确保你们得到公正的审判。
E: Intruders, give it up! You will be guaranteed a fair trial.

C: …危险？
E: ...Not safe?

C: 什么操作空间？
E: What wiggle room?

C: 嗯？这里好像什么也没有啊……
E: Huh? Looks like there's nothing here...

C: 既然它写的是「踩我」…那我们就按它说的做吧。
E: Well, it says "stomp on me"... So, let's do just that.

C: 总之，这个箭头图案应该就是进一步的线索——只是我也不太清楚这意味着什么…我们还是跟着查宝接着找其他线索吧。
E: Anyway, this arrow pattern must be another clue — but I'm not sure what this means... Let's follow Findie and look for other clues.

C: 我们先分开行动吧。筑城领域中有着和铁卫禁区中同样构造的旋转栈桥，我得去调查一下它的控制终端。
E: Let's split up for now. In the Fortified Zone, there is a revolving gear bridge that has the same structure as the one in the Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone. I have to go there to investigate its control terminal.

C: 哼哼，开个玩笑而已。
E: Haha, I was just kidding.

C: 「炉心」通路重新开放之后，我心里就萌生了一个想法。我给养父母的老宅地址写了一封信，拜托桑博替我送达。
E: After the reopening of the Furnace Core passage, I had an idea. I wrote a letter and asked Sampo to deliver it to my parents' old address.

C: 每年他们的结婚纪念日，爸爸都会送妈妈一束红纸包扎的「球牡丹」。
E: Each year for their anniversary, my dad would send my mom a bouquet of Ball Peonies wrapped in red paper.

C: 我们家在医学院的人脉很深，不管你需要的是药物或是文献资料，应该都不成问题。
E: We have many connections in the medical academy. No matter what you need, be it medicine or data, I should be able to get it for you.

C: 娜塔莎小姐！请留步！
E: Miss Natasha! One moment please!

C: 他没必要在信里对我撒谎。当然也有一种可能，或许在写下这些文字的时候，他已经彻底疯了……
E: There'd be no point in him lying to me in the letters. Still, it's possible he may have gone completely insane before he started writing them...

C: 但哪怕出自「恶魔」之手，这些「风雪免疫」还是可以造福后人。
E: But maybe this Blizzard Immunity can now bring benefit to others... even if it was made by a monster.

C: 有小偷？
E: There's a thief on the prowl?

C: 停，停下！你想干嘛——
E: Wait! What are you doing!?

C: 呵呵，年轻人真会说话！
E: Hehe, young people these days sure know how to talk.

C: 等等，我还有急事要处理。
E: Hold on, I have something urgent to take care of first.

C: 我尚未理清事态的脉络，但如果你们的行为帮助到了{NICKNAME}和{F#她}{M#他}的同伴——
E: I am unable to determine the logic behind your actions, but if you managed to help {NICKNAME} and {F#her}{M#his} companions...

C: 呼…呼…真是费了好大的力气，早知道就再多叫几个帮手了……
E: *sigh* If I'd known how much work that was going to be, I would've enlisted more help...

C: 我的武器？送你了。
E: Was it my weapon? Here — I'll give it to you right now.

C: 咳…这、这不是重点。
E: Uhh... th—that's beside the point.

C: 这样啊…我明白了，不好意思占用了你的时间。
E: I see, sorry for taking up your time.

C: 猛地一下破门而入！
E: Break down the door with a bang!

C: 那…老桑博就先走一步了，祝你跟你的朋友都能度过开心的今天、明天、还有后天！
E: Well, I'll be on my way then. I wish you and your friends a happy today, a happy tomorrow, and a happy day after that!

C: 好的。那我们就开始吧，娜塔莎小姐。
E: Alright. Let's get started then, Miss Natasha.

C: 没想到您还见过我的母亲……
E: I never expected you to have met my mom...

C: 所以…可能要占用{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}一点时间了，非常抱歉！
E: So... this is going to take up some of your time, {M#Mister}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}.

C: 很有个性！就这个吧。
E: Sounds cool to me! Let's use it.

C: 嘿，既然连你都这么说，那我就放心了。
E: Hee-hee, glad to hear that, your words give me confidence.

C: 那么，希露瓦小姐……
E: Well, Miss Serval...

C: 我也意识到这个问题了……
E: I'm aware of this problem too...

C: 佩拉…啊，我记得，是铁卫的那个情报官姑娘。
E: Pela... Oh, I remember her. She's the Intelligence Officer of the Silvermane Guard.

C: 可恶……
E: Darn it...

C: 好了，面试到此为止。接下来，我来公布面试结果——
E: Well, let's call it a day. I'll announce the result now.

C: 那么，我先带着她去办手续，先失陪了。以后也要常来博物馆里看看——当然，主要是来看看希儿的工作状态哦。
E: Please excuse me now, as I have to take Seele to complete the procedure. Remember to visit the museum often and check on Seele.

C: 你就别动弹了，娜塔莎医生让你好好休息，你这伤口又裂开了怎么办？再说了，伊蕾恩会把他带出来的，那姑娘靠谱。
E: Easy, Dr. Natasha told you to rest. What if your wound opens up again? Besides, Elaine will get him out, she's a reliable girl.

C: 还好底盘跟轮对保存得还算完整。帮我一把，把这俩轮子卸下来。
E: At least the chassis and the wheelset are relatively intact. Here, give me a hand removing the wheels.

C: 怎么办，小偷好像把老爹的宝贝带走了…这根断掉的探针，好像就是探测器上的！
E: I think the thief took daddy's treasure... and I think that this damaged probe is from the detector!

C: 矿区里有很多坏人，娜塔莎姐姐说了，小孩子不能随便进去！你快替我们去看看！
E: There's lots of bad guys in the mines. Miss Natasha said kids ain't allowed to go in! Go check on her for us now!

C: 你把矿脉的消息卖给了那个疯婆子安东尼娜！明明是我们先…现在好了，我们还得反过来给那群家伙打工！
E: You sold the intel about the vein to Antonia, but we discovered it first! Now, we lost the mine and we have to work for those guys!

C: 是我在矿坑里捡到的古代遗物，据说是以前的开采团专门拿来探测地髓矿脉的。
E: It's an ancient relic that I found in a mine. People say it's a device that mining teams used for detecting Geomarrow veins long ago.

C: 说谎的人就是真正的小偷。
E: The liar must be the thief.

C: 我相信你，但是我说了不算。
E: I believe you, but it's not up to me to judge.

C: 现在还不能下断言。
E: We shouldn't jump to conclusions yet.

C: 仔细看看！一定要帮虎克修好它！
E: Yes, look extra close! You have to help Hook fix it!

C: 有事才找我，把我当工具人？
E: You only come to me when you need help. You think I'm a robot?

C: 裸露的地表上立着些矿具，从表面的锈迹来看，似乎是矿工们多年来在下班路上积攒出的「成果」。
E: There are some mining tools left standing on the ground. The rust stains on the ground seem to be the result of miners dragging their tools on their way home over the years.

C: 向炮手各单位传令——坚守阵地，向步战单位前进方向提供徐进弹幕支援。
E: Put all artillery units on standby. Wait for my command to bombard enemy positions and cover our infantry advance.

C: 咦？怎么了？
E: Huh? What is it?
...██ █Landau██ █ █ stand down!

C: …哈，真的吗？
E: ...Ha, really?

C: 原来如此…那我们还是继续向前找找吧。
E: I see... Then let's keep moving.

C: 是的，这种兔子的外形特别像雪地萝卜——耳朵是叶，身子是茎。它冬眠的时候会用土块包裹身体，看上去就和地里的萝卜没有任何区别了。
E: Yep. They look really similar to snow radishes. Their ears look like the leaves and their body looks like the radish bit. When they hibernate, they cover themselves with dirt, just like real radishes.

C: 呃…{M#他}{F#她}就是那个拯救了贝洛伯格的大英雄{NICKNAME}。
E: Um... This is the trailblazer — the savior of Belobog.

C: 哥哥，姐姐……
E: Serval, Gepard...

C: 那边，远处山坡上的那幢房子，就是娜塔莎以前的孤儿院。
E: Way over there, see that house on the hill? That's Natasha's old orphanage.

C: 泥土的味道还很新鲜，对方应该没离开太久。
E: *sniff* The scent of dirt is still fresh. They can't have come too long before us.

C: 但愿物资没被刚才那家伙毁掉。
E: I just hope the monster didn't get to them.

C: 保护克拉拉。开启主动防卫模式——
E: Protect Clara. Initiating active defense mode—

C: 帕金斯，不要！快停下！
E: Perkins, don't! Stop!

C: 喂，你别到处乱跑！先在孤儿院这边找找吧。
E: Hey, where are you going!? Let's look around the orphanage first.

C: 唉，遇到你这么懒的人，虎克也是倒了霉了。
E: *sigh* Why are you so lazy? I'm so unlucky!

C: 老巫婆说过，「菌菌」可不是一般的玩偶！只要把它打理干净，肯定会很抢手的！
E: Well, the old witch said that Junjun isn't just any old teddy bear! He'll be worth a lot of money once he's cleaned up!

C: 呼…真扫兴，我还以为他会趁这个机会对我说些什么呢……
E: Hmph, so he is a scaredy-cat. I knew he'd get someone else to talk to me...
█ ██Language module█connect█connecting███ ██ █ ██—

C: 完美的结局！该结算奖励了。
E: A happy ending! We should work out our rewards.

C: 零件，存放！敌人，躲避。地方，安全。害怕，不用，大家！
E: Parts, store! Enemies, not here. Place, safe. Scared, no need, everybody!

C: 克拉拉需要好好想想…{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，我们再把帕斯卡的事从头到尾理一下吧？
E: Let me think... How about we go over Pascal's whole story again, from the start?

C: 先礼后兵，还是先考虑和平沟通吧。
E: Diplomacy before violence. We should try peaceful communication first.

C: 哈哈哈，很好！不愧是要接过守护者担子的姑娘，觉悟很高啊！
E: Hahaha, excellent! An enlightened answer, young lady — and spoken like a true successor to the guardianship.

C: 哎呀呀，这地方可真是泥沙俱下，啥人都有。招子放亮点伙计们，别被这些家伙给骗了啊。
E: Alas, this place is a bit of a mixed bag — plenty of... "characters." Keep your wits about you, friends. Don't let anyone deceive you.

C: 我告诉你们吧，这道大门的机制我已经搞清楚了。可不是什么阿猫阿狗都能随便出入史瓦罗大佬的地盘，想见他的人，必须拿到「认证」。
E: Luckily for us, I've already figured out the mechanism for this gate. They don't just let any Joe Shmoe in and out of Boss Svarog's territory. I mean, if you wanna see the big cheese himself, you need "certification."

C: 回答错误。认证结果——失败。
E: Incorrect answer. Certification result: Failure.

C: 八十八度矿底
E: <unbreak>88</unbreak> Degrees Bedrock

C: 我…知道了。我会带大家去见史瓦罗先生。
E: I... Okay. I'll take you to see Mr. Svarog.

C: 指令已收到。门禁终端已解锁。
E: Command received. Access restriction terminal unlocked.

C: 我们为地下的未来而来。
E: We're here about the future of the underground.

C: 与其说是紧张，不如说是…胸口有什么东西，在剧烈作痛。
E: No, but... I do have an aching feeling in my chest.

C: 找到了，守护者大人——所有文献都在这里了。
E: Madam Guardian, I've found them... All the documents are here.

C: 桑博…我们到了没啊？
E: Sampo... Are we there yet?

C: 这拍卖会都是什么人参加？
E: Who's going to the auction?

C: （歌蒂在找适合小孩子的玩具？或许可以说服她也来竞价虎克的玩偶熊。）
E: (So Gertie's hunting for children's toys? Maybe we can persuade her to bid on Hook's teddy bear too.)

C: 我才是你的{F#孙女}{M#孙子}啊！
E: I'M your grandchild!

C: 好，就抢{F#她们}{M#他们}！上啊！
E: Agreed. Get them!

C: 要是奶奶拍到了，别告诉你爸妈这是奶奶买的，就说是这位{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}送给你的吧。
E: If grandma wins the auction, don't tell your parents I bought it! Let's pretend it was a gift from this kind {F#young lady}{M#young man}.

C: 那个工作人员就在那边，我们赶紧去问问吧！
E: Let's go ask that staff member!

C: 哦，我知道了——其实你们只是想独吞探针，是吧？嗐，我还以为自己碰上的是什么正经人，没想到是被黑吃黑了……
E: Oh... I see. You just wanna keep the probe to yourselves, right? *sigh* And here I was thinking I'd met some nice people. You're worse than I am...

C: 呜…呜，老爹的探针……
E: *sob* Daddy's Geomarrow Probe...

C: 想想有没有别的礼物可以送吧。
E: Aren't there any other gifts you could send him?

C: 我本来也没想出手…但我可不想看到你们两个垂头丧气的样子啊。
E: I wasn't planning on saying anything... but I saw a couple of very sad-looking faces in the crowd.

C: 怎么样，虎克？愿意把我手上的地髓探针买走吗？
E: What do you think, Hook? Are you willing to buy the Geomarrow Probe from me?

C: 有了它，老爹肯定就能把「矿脉探测器」修好了吧？
E: With this, you should be able to repair the vein detector, right daddy?

C: 费斯曼也不会想当这个冤大头吧？不过我可以去问问，能出手的话还是尽早出了才好……
E: Fersman wouldn't want to be the sucker, would he? But maybe I should ask him... It would be great to get rid of it as early as possible...

C: 瞧你这样…不会是共犯吧！
E: Why are you being so evasive? Are you an accomplice?

C: 啊，不过它好像确实拿着叔叔阿姨们丢失的零件——
E: It looks like it's carrying the vagrants' missing parts...

C: 是啊，可是为什么……？
E: Yes, but why?

C: 唔…那可不行！克拉拉不能接受……
E: Absolutely not! I'm not going to give up...

C: 史瓦罗先生，我们回来啦。
E: Mr. Svarog, we're back.

C: 好像是呢…但克拉拉听不明白。
E: It appears so. I'm not sure what he's trying to say though.

C: 基地网络会将扇区对应储存的信息完整备份，并在清除信息噪音时将完整信息覆写至对应区域。
E: When the muddled information is removed, the base network will create a comprehensive backup and overwrite the data stored in the corresponding sector.

C: 正在接入个体。准备备份该个体核心模组内全部信息。
E: Accessing. Preparing to back up all core module data.

C: 克服不了困难，谈什么开拓。
E: Without hardships to overcome, there is no trailblazing.

C: 哼，咳！萨克雷，你几天没洗澡了？
E: Hmm? *cough* Zackery, how many days has it been since you showered?

C: 死板的家伙…但很能打。「地火」缺的就是这种人。
E: Stubborn fool... strong, though. Wildfire lacks people like him.

C: 你没发现我们不管想说服谁，结果都是要打一顿吗？
E: Haven't you noticed yet? Most of our "convincing" ends in a fight.

C: 你们将所知道的一切告知杰帕德，并交出了布洛妮娅的信件。
E: You tell Gepard everything you know and show him Bronya's letter.

C: 你看，姐弟间的矛盾就是这样，痛快打上一架之后就和好如初啦。
E: You see? Brothers and sisters need to have a scrap once in a while to get back on good terms.

C: 难道我的梦想真的是个笑话吗？什么公正平等的城市，在这里压根就不可能实现……
E: Is my dream really a joke? A city that stands for justice and equality... it's impossible in this world!

C: 这里到处都是铁锈和烟尘的味道…我不太喜欢。
E: It reeks of rust and soot here... I don't like it one bit.

C: 等等…我好像…还听到了一些声音，是从人群中发出的声音。
E: Wait... I think I can... hear a voice, a voice coming out of the crowd.

C: 多长个心眼，不要随便碰裂界里的东西。
E: Be careful, think twice before you interact with anything in the Fragmentum.

C: 嘿，理智如丹恒，也有向冒险精神妥协的一天嘛！
E: Ha, even Dan Heng the sensible has his adventurous days!

C: 但来自异世界的军团不知疲倦。她很快就意识到，血肉之躯根本不可能与如此可怕的敌人抗衡。
E: But the otherworldly Legion did not tire. She soon realized that flesh and blood could not contend with so ruthless an enemy.

C: ——而不是把命运交给这枚祸种！
E: ...Not hand over our fate to this seed of ruin!

C: 但那虚卒的遗体竟保存得如此完整，你不禁开始思考…也许三月七的怀疑有些道理。
E: The Voidranger remains are preserved remarkably well in the ice. You can't help but wonder... perhaps March 7th is right.

C: 我只是不知道要怎么让所有人接受这一切……
E: I don't know whether I can get the people to accept all this...

C: 那就这么办吧，娜塔那边就交给你们了。为了布洛…不对，是为了整个贝洛伯格——麻烦你们，保守好这个秘密。
E: That's settled then. I'll leave Nat to you. Remember to keep the secret — for Bron— No, for Belobog's sake.

C: 我失足踏入那敞开的箱门，以注定被世人耻笑的方式结束了自己的一生。
E: I slipped and fell into that open box, ending my life in a way that's destined for ridicule.

C: （原来不明信号只是「星际和平家电」的推销信息…把这件事告诉杨叔吧。）
E: (So, the unknown signal was actually an ad from the Interastral Peace Appliances... I'll let Mr. Yang know.)

C: 她应该是这颗星球上最接近历史真相之人。去向她请教一下，或许会有不少收获呢。
E: She is likely the person closest to the true history of the planet. Go and ask her. You might learn something.

C: 「星际和平公司」…这个名字，我好像在守护者的历史教材里读到过。似乎是个在星际间拥有巨大影响力的组织，还和「存护」有关……
E: Interastral Peace Corporation... I've read about it before in the Guardians' history textbook. I think it's some kind of interstellar organization that has massive influences all over the cosmos. It has something to do with Preservation...

C: 嗯…也是。不过就算将来取得联系，对贝洛伯格应该也是利大于弊……
E: Hmmm... maybe you're right. But I still believe they might be a net positive for Belobog if there comes a time when we do make contact with them in the future.

C: {NICKNAME}总是不负众望呢。看来，以后遇到什么事都可以放心交给你啦。那么，这次终于可以把奖励……
E: You never let us down, {NICKNAME}. I suppose if we ever have a problem, we can just leave it in your capable hands. Now then, we can finally give that reward to...

C: 比如我会问你<color=#dbc291ff>「蓝色的客运型星槎有多少艘？」</color>，需要根据你的观察，正确地回答我的问题。
E: For example, I might ask <color=#dbc291ff>"How many blue passenger starskiffs went past?"</color> You will need to answer my questions according to your observation.

C: 别在这里磨叽了，你快去抓人。
E: Enough talk. You go catch the culprit.

C: 肇事者已经跑了。
E: The perpetrator ran away.

C: 好像有什么纸片飞出来了…
E: Looks like some kind of paper flew out...

C: 没有……
E: No...

C: 我想想，我先是听到奇怪的引擎声，然后撞上了，我跑了出来，它消失不见了…重点还是那个引擎。
E: Let me think. First, I heard some weird engine noises, and then the collision. I ran outside, but it has already disappeared... The most important part is still the engine.

C: 别的东西我都忘记了，就记得一件事，那艘星槎到了十字航道之后，<color=#dbc291ff>朝右边拐过去了！</color>我记得很清楚。
E: I do remember this, though. When the starskiff arrived at the crossroad, <color=#dbc291ff>it made a right turn there!</color> I remember that very clearly.

C: 芦慧一直在担心你。
E: Luhui is worried about you.

C: 西口的机巧鸟。
E: Cycrane at the west entrance.

C: 北口的机巧鸟
E: Cycrane at the north entrance

C: 你听过我抱怨了很多次梅姐，但是她给我翻看她的小本子的时候，记下了很多「我说过的话」，我才知道自己也不算是一个好的雇主。
E: You've heard me complain about Mei many times. I didn't know that I was not a good employer until I saw her notebook. She has written down so many things that I said.

C: 寻找丢失的笔记，「谛听」应该能派上用场……
E: Looking for a missing notebook, Diting might come in handy...

C: 等等，绕了一大圈，你好像很了解丹鼎司的内部情况嘛！我听说罗浮将军破天荒地邀请了几位化外民参赞内务…难不成就是你？
E: Wait... I've been looking everywhere, but you seem to know a lot about what's happening inside the Alchemy Commission! I heard that the general of the Luofu invited some outworlders to get involved in their internal affairs in a rare turn of events... Could you be one of those people?

C: 你帮了我这么多忙，我也不是个什么小气的人。除了看得见摸得着的赏金外，我还提供博识学会最常见的报酬，知识——知识远胜黄金。
E: You've helped me so much, and I'm definitely not a stingy person. Apart from tangible cash, I will also provide the most common reward in the Intelligentsia Guild — that of knowledge. After all, knowledge is worth more than gold.

C: …你还是别写诗了。
E: ...You're not great at this poetry business.

C: 咳，我写诗…是为了赢得一个女孩的芳心。
E: Well, I write poetry... to win the heart of a fair maiden.

C: 流离宾至浮槎落，彷徨仙临徂逝川。罗浮亲友如相问，一片冰心在玉壶 / 星河重逢终有时 / 吃了上顿没下顿。你确定要这样写吗？
E: I travel on a raft as a wandering stranger, drifting along as on a divine river. Should those who know me on the Luofu query my life, I'll say my heart is still as pure and righteous as jade. / I'll say our paths shall reconvene among the stars / I'll say I'm living from hand to mouth. Are you sure this is what you want to write?

C: 你有什么喜欢的东西吗？
E: Do you have anything you like?

C: 我忙得很，你要说什么来着？
E: I'm busy, what do you want to tell me?

C: 我不相信，黛青小姐姐的聊天频道有哪些牛鬼蛇神我还不清楚吗？你就别装了。
E: I don't believe you. I'm so familiar with everyone in Ms. Daiqing's chat channel, you can't deceive me.

C: 我很怀疑这样的决定，但既然你要求了，我试试吧。
E: I highly doubt this decision, but since you insisted it, I'll try.

C: 别做梦了，怎么能让你们打扰到她呢？
E: Are you guys out of your minds? How could I allow anyone to disturb Ms. Daiqing?

C: 其实我能找到黛青在哪里。
E: Actually, I have a way to find Daiqing.

C: 真的吗，那个狐人姑娘也在流云渡吗…怎么都喜欢来码头啊？又不是什么警匪片。
E: Really, that foxian girl is also at Cloudford? Why would she go all the way out to the port? What is this, some kind of mafia movie?

C: {NICKNAME}，帮帮老陶德的事情，你考虑的怎么样了？
E: {NICKNAME}, have you decided whether to help me?

C: 陶德接收了你拍摄的照片，一张一张细细端详。
E: Todd takes the pictures from you and scrutinizes them one by one.

C: 涂鸦古迹，不文明！
E: It's uncivilized to vandalize a historical monument!

C: （这么说…只要将相机<color=#dbc291ff>调整到合适的角度</color>，就能将标识<color=#dbc291ff>拼合</color>在一起……）
E: (So... I just have to get the Camera <color=#dbc291ff>adjusted to the right angle</color> and I'll be able to get the markings <color=#dbc291ff>pieced together</color>...)

C: 不知陶德是否真的得到了自己想要的一切……
E: I wonder if Todd has truly gotten everything he wanted...

C: 需要我请丹鼎司的医生吗？
E: Do you want me to fetch a healer from the Alchemy Commission?

C: 只是一张普通的家庭合照，没有什么疑点。
E: An ordinary family photo, nothing to see here...

C: 【金色的徽章】
E: Golden Badge

C: 唉唉，用不着这么拘礼。本小姐跑出来的时候就是个与民同乐的食客。初次见面，你袋袋里就没带着点零食什么的，快快献上大家一起口福同享？
E: Please, no need for formalities. I came out here just to be another diner among the people. You don't happen to have any snacks in your bag, do you? Why don't you share with us what you've got?

C: 打记事起我就在学医，从来没人问过我喜欢不喜欢，持明族那些老东西们不在乎，我也不在乎。
E: I've been studying medicine for as long as I can remember. Nobody's ever asked me whether I like it. Those old Vidyadhara fools don't care, and neither do I.

C: 我们只是一般路过的游客。
E: We're just passing tourists.

C: {F#小妹妹}{M#小兄弟}，还记得我之前说过的话吗，万一到了那个时候……
E: Hey, do you still remember what I said earlier? If the time comes...

C: 她有没有托付给你们什么秘密的盒子呀、信件什么的？
E: Did she give you any secret boxes or letters?

C: 那么，我希望您不要和白露小姐见面。
E: Then, I hope you don't plan on meeting with Miss Bailu.

C: 但是，丹恒大人若将一切交由白露小姐继承，恐将惹来巨大的风波，对她也绝无好处。
E: But, if Master Dan Heng were to hand everything over to Miss Bailu, it could lead to great upheavals on the horizon. No good would come of it for her.

C: 哈哈，那与我何干？我是受龙女照拂，在丹鼎司疗伤养病，赶巧撞上两位。
E: Haha, this encounter was not of my making. I've been recuperating in the Alchemy Commission under the Dragon Lady's care — our paths have merely crossed...

C: 而今丹恒回归，长老又看到了一个机会，岂能放过？
E: Now that Dan Heng has arrived, the elders see yet another chance — how could they let it go?

C: 出发吧。
E: Let's go.

C: 是丹鼎司的刺客？
E: Is it an assassin from the Alchemy Commission?

C: 快看，那边飘动的影子…该不会是刺客吧？
E: Look — look at that shadow... Could it be an assassin?

C: 不可能，我…我还活着！龙尊传承绝不能在这一代中断……
E: Impossible, I—I still live! The high elder succession will not be severed in this generation...

C: 为什么您一语不发？大人。您还是像过去一样清冷孤独，就像是方壶仙舟上万载不化的玄冰。
E: Why do you remain silent? Your Highness, you're still as cold and lonely as you ever were, like the eternal ice on the Fanghu.

C: 你必须交出来！
E: Hand it over!

C: 是我，抱歉，白露小姐。芫梨再也做不了您的侍女了，不过，我听说有名精明能干的侍女会来接替我。
E: It's me, Miss Bailu. I'm so sorry that I can't be your maid anymore, but I heard that a smart and talented replacement would be coming soon.

C: …芫梨，你放心，我从未告诉过任何人梦境的事。
E: ...Yuanli, don't worry. I never told anyone about my dreams.

C: 你有兴趣再重温一下吗？
E: Would you like to re-visit them?

C: 谢谢您！对不住，耽误您时间了，我们先走了。
E: I don't know what to say! Sorry to trouble you — we'll take our leave now.

C: 看看影像二。
E: Watch footage two.

C: 《渔公案》啊，就那本侦探小说。里面有一幕和这段影像一模一样。
E: The Angler Mystery — the novel! There's a scene that's identical to this footage.

C: 芸慎大哥，我有点急事。你先帮我看一下店，我马上回来。隐书
E: Hi Yunshen! I have an errand to run, please watch the store for me while I'm gone. Back soon — Yinshu

C: 你们去找人吧，我留在这里等等看。仙舟上很多书籍似乎挺有意思的，我也趁此机会了解一下。
E: You two go ahead, I'll wait here. The Xianzhou has some interesting literature — I'd like to get better acquainted.

C: 他还讹了我们五百镝呢，纯纯的坏家伙。
E: That crook cheated us out of <unbreak>500</unbreak> strales.

C: 哎！你怎么就收下了！隐书，这不是你的错，你不用这样的。
E: Hey, you're gonna take it!? Yinshu, this isn't your fault — you don't have to do this.

C: （说好让杨叔请客的，我可不能越俎代庖。嘿嘿。）
E: (I think it was Mr. Yang who promised us Xianzhou delicacies earlier? Wouldn't wanna steal his thunder, hee-hee...)

C: 我知道你救人心切，但只有查清现场的情况，才方便救援。时间耽搁不起了，咱们动身吧。
E: I know you want to help, but we need more intel on the crash site before we start the rescue. There's no time to lose — let's go.

C: 我想做最后一次努力。如果即使这样，妈妈都不同意的话…那我也只好甘愿做一个天舶司的文员了。
E: I want to try one last time. If Mom still doesn't agree... then I'll resign myself to signing documents in the Sky-Faring Commission.

C: 这是…采翼的日记？这东西怎么会在……
E: Is that... Caiyi's diary? How did you...?

C: 我想…和您一起飞行一次……
E: I want to... fly with you at least once...

C: 等你们做完要做的事情，再来这里找我吧。
E: Come find me once you've done what you're here to do.

C: 我留下来帮你。
E: I'll stay and help you.

C: （说谎）我不在乎你。
E: (Lie) I don't care about you.

C: （云骑似乎都被那台金人吸引了，就别上去凑热闹了。）
E: (The Cloud Knights are all distracted by the aurumaton. I should just stay away from the commotion.)

C: 基因上来说，你是我的孩子。构成你身体的数据有四分之一来自于我。不过，银狼、阿刃和萨姆都不适合带孩子。
E: Genetically speaking, you are my child. A quarter of the data that makes up your body comes from me. Of course, neither Silver Wolf, Bladie, nor Sam are well-suited for looking after a child...

C: 我的暗示能让人心甘情愿地接受指令，甚至自己都察觉不到异样。
E: My powers of suggestion are capable of convincing people to accept my orders... all without a second thought.

C: 从找到开启玉界门的人入手。如果此人别有用心，正好替我们省点麻烦；如果是工作人员，就从对方那里问出发生了什么。
E: We should start with the person who opened the Jade Gate. If it turns out they're not friendly, then at least they've already made things easier for us. If it turns out they were just doing their job, then we can ask them what happened here.

C: 哎呀～方才是我失言，恩公不要往心里去啦。你瞧，前面的路都给堵住了。
E: *sigh* I shouldn't have doubted you, please accept my apologies. Our road ahead is barricaded, do you see?

C: 这船是通往哪里的？
E: Where does it go?

C: 说不上认得，只是有些面熟，是将军的客人？
E: I can't say that I know her, but she does look a little familiar. Is she a guest of the general's?

C: 但你无法逃离战斗，丹恒，你的枪术与饮月所用的技艺如出一辙。
E: But you cannot run from combat, Dan Heng. Your spearmanship is identical to Imbibitor Lunae's.

C: 她该不会想故意差走我吧…
E: Did she get rid of me on purpose...

C: 我双眼无碍，将它蒙上只是不愿睹物思旧，坠入心魔。我最近神思纷乱，时有夜梦惊悸，想请龙女大人瞧瞧，可有安神的法子。
E: My eyes are fine. I cover them to avoid seeing that which reminds me of the past and riles my inner demons. My thoughts have been disordered lately, and I experience occasional night terrors. Do you have a way to soothe my mind, Dragon Lady?

C: 唉，我尽力吧。接下来不管我要开什么药，她都得多喝热水才行。
E: ...I'll do my best. No matter what I administer, she's gonna need to drink a ton of warm water from this point on.

C: 碍事的东西，我会为龙女顺手除去。我的剑寒凛冽，请站远些。
E: Allow me to eliminate these obstacles, Dragon Lady... Stand back, the chill of my sword freezes everything in its path.

C: 放弃吧…
E: Just give up...

C: 三月七！
E: March 7th!

C: 可恶，为什么是{NICKNAME}收到了小谛听的呼叫？难道不应该是咱这样的美少女更容易得到小猫小狗的信任吗？
E: Hmph! Why is Diting talking to you? I've always been nice to cute little animals!

C: 你该不会…有谛听血脉吧？
E: You wouldn't happen to be... related to Diting?

C: 调查
E: Investigate

C: 事起仓促，我正在亲自追捕一名潜逃的要犯，为了防止失联，我会留下这枚「示迹玉扣」记录行踪。
E: I'm in active pursuit of a fugitive. The situation is unpredictable and developing rapidly, so I've planted this jade tracer to record my whereabouts in case I lose contact.

C: 我能听到。飞剑破空的鸣动，锐锋切割的声响…这些痕迹都会在无形中流露出剑艺的优劣。
E: I heard it. A sword whistles through the air and rings out upon contacting the enemy ... Whilst invisible to the eye, such signals reveal the quality of one's swordplay.

C: 剑芒未出，怎知胜负高下？劝你别小瞧我的剑。
E: Hmph, we've not even drawn swords yet and you declare yourself the winner? Let me offer you a word of advice — don't underestimate me.

C: 太卜整天吹嘘自己未卜先知。这回的祸事算出来了吗？
E: The master diviner is always boasting about her foresight. Didn't manage to foresee this disaster, did she?

C: 从哪儿来…
E: (Where did she come from...?)

C: 这是……？
E: What's this...?

C: …好吧，看来小爷非得给你个教训不可了。
E: "...Little one"? This little one is about to teach you a big lesson.

C: 对了，这一路上，您有什么发现吗？
E: Speaking of which, have you made any discoveries so far?

C: （先用手机留下一份副本，然后拿去交给绿芙蓉吧。）
E: (I'll use my phone to make a copy and then hand it over to Green Hibiscus.)

C: 关于药王秘传的现状…
E: Tell me about the current state of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus...

C: 你的气味很明显。失去一种感官，会让其它的感觉被迫变得敏锐。
E: You have a very distinct scent. Losing one sense forces the other senses to become sharper.

C: 咱们尽快找到星槎渡口，把这位十王司判官送走，好接着赶咱们的路。
E: I suggest we hasten to Starskiff Jetty and send this Judge of the Ten-Lords Commission on her way — we must continue our own journeys as soon as possible.

C: 噢，那确实，可能见不到是最好的……
E: Oh, hehe... Hopefully you'll be the last one I meet for a while then...

C: 那怎么办？
E: Any ideas?

C: 不必担心。受穷观阵影响时，大部分人会因为过量的信息冲刷而感到晕眩。
E: As expected. Most individuals feel nauseous after being presented with the dizzying excess of information provided by the Matrix.

C: 谁啊，怎么忍心对我下这样的毒手啊！
E: Ah!? Who would do such a horrible mean thing!?

C: 少废话，给我摸。
E: Enough of that — touch it.

C: 这大概是我脑补出来的…主要是我也不记得在贝洛伯格喝过什么有名的好茶了。
E: My brain must be filling in the blanks... Probably because I never drank any famous tea in Belobog.

C: 在宾馆里美美地睡了一觉，醒来发现自己变成了通缉犯…印象确实是很深。
E: Well, waking up after a great sleep in a comfy bed to find out you're a wanted criminal... tends to leave a deep impression.

C: 三月，清醒一点，我们要抓紧些了。
E: March, wake up — we have to hurry.

C: 聊聊卡勒瓦拉。
E: Let's talk about Kalevala.

C: 那是一柄「魔剑」，你听说过「魔剑」吗？
E: Have you ever heard of cursed swords? That sword is one.

C: {NICKNAME}，我那孙女没有惹出什么事吧？
E: My granddaughter didn't cause any trouble, did she, {NICKNAME}?

C: 啊，是密室凶案…
E: Ah, it's like a locked-room murder mystery...

C: 那你…偷了吗？
E: So... did you steal it?

C: 我其实正在想，如果我找到了魔剑，是不是就算是直接洗脱了嫌疑？
E: If I can find that cursed sword, I can prove my innocence right away.

C: 你合上剑匣，将它放回原处。
E: You close the sword case and put it back.

C: 我既不能留在武库中说服工匠，也不愿意如同窃贼一般带走宝剑。为此我留下了一个代表我身份的小小证据，希望能与各位开诚布公地谈一谈。
E: I couldn't stay in the arsenal to convince the craftsmen, nor did I want to take the sword like a thief. That's why I left behind a small piece of evidence that pointed to my identity in the hope of an open and honest conversation with you.

C: 你说「仙舟人余生太长了」…没错，我打算用接下来的岁月培育一枚种子，名为「反抗」的种子，我要让这片土地的人们懂得拿起武器去反抗那些怪物。
E: You said that the rest of a Xianzhou native's life could be terribly long... You're right. That's why I intend to plant a seed named "resistance" here and nurture it for the rest of my life. I want to show the people of this land how to defend themselves against those monsters.

C: 仙舟人的余生还很长，会有新的年轻人站出来和我一起战斗的！时间站在我们这边。
E: The rest of this Xianzhou native's life is still terribly long, and there will be new youngsters who will stand beside me in battle! Time is on our side.

C: 出发吧。
E: Let's go.

C: 对了，卡勒瓦拉的使节和那位骑士一同去了金人巷。他们打算挑一处地方纪念英雄。咱们一起去瞧瞧吧。
E: By the way, the ambassador from Kalevala and that knight went to Aurum Alley. They're looking for a spot to honor the hero. Why don't we go take a look together?

C: 即便在我认识的愚者中，花火小姐的幽默感也别具一格呢。
E: Even among the fools I know, Miss Sparkle's sense of humor stands out from the rest.

C: 真是可爱的答案呢。
E: What a cute answer.

C: 竟然不行吗？看来花火小姐比我想象中还要「严谨」一些。
E: It doesn't? Looks like Miss Sparkle is more careful than I thought.

C: 请问副警长小姐还在吗？
E: Excuse me, is the deputy sheriff still around?

C: 侦探和侦探助手成了两具颓然的死物。看来，它们再也无法完成任务了。
E: The detective and the assistant detective have become two lifeless bodies. It seems they can no longer complete their mission.

C: 我们来办，不算窥探机密吧？
E: Won't we be breaching Xianzhou secrets in the process?

C: 最容易上当受骗的是你吧！
E: Nah, you're the one more likely to get fooled.

C: 你们的剧本是…杀死星神？
E: So your plan is... to kill an Aeon?

C: 所以我并没有说「星神」会被人类杀死，不是吗？
E: ...but then I didn't bring humans into the picture, did I?

C: …等一下，时间应该差不多了。
E: ...Hold on, any minute now.

C: 卡芙卡跑了？可恶，这下怎么向那位太卜交代啦！
E: Did Kafka escape? Argh, how are we gonna explain this to the master diviner!?

C: 你是说…将军？
E: You mean... the general?

C: 青雀小姐的好意，在下心领了。打牌的事，只能等下次有机会了。
E: Your kindness is much appreciated, Miss Qingque. As for celestial jade, we'll have to leave it for next time.

C: {NICKNAME}说的好。多谢太卜大人的吉言。出发吧。
E: Well said, {NICKNAME}. Thank you for your assurance, Master Diviner. Onward.

C: 噢，瞧这身打扮…白高兴一场，原来是旅客啊。
E: ...Your clothes. *sigh* I knew I was getting ahead of myself — you're just tourists.

C: …他干嘛要跑回去啊？
E: ...Why did he run back in?

C: 你们看，「建木」的根系……
E: Look, the Ambrosial Arbor's roots...

C: 伤到哪里都能复原…这也太离谱了吧！
E: No matter how much damage we do, it just heals itself... not fair!

C: 第四、老大从不会哭鼻子。就算看到了他因为被他爹打屁股或者被黉学先生罚站而痛哭流涕，也必须要装作没看到！
E: Number four, the boss never cries. Sure, his dad might yell at him sometimes, and the lyceum abecedaries like to make him stand in the hallway, but those are tears of strength — not weakness!

C: 你们俩吵什么！谁不知道我才是银河第一恶棍！
E: What are you guys talking about!? Obviously, I'm the baddest baddy in the galaxy!

C: 谁来救救我……
E: Somebody help me...

C: 你了解药王秘传吗？
E: Do you know much about the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus?

C: 「餐云承露丹」会将你转变为长生种，并让你体会慈怀药王为血肉之躯带来的无限自由。但你似乎无福消受啊。
E: The Brumedew Concentrate Pellet will turn you into a long-life species — you will feel the limitless freedom of the form bestowed upon you by Merciful Medicus... By not taking it, you are foregoing its benefits.

C: 所以…真遗憾啊，如果你先认识的是在下，也许我们就能成为真正的朋友了。
E: Most unfortunate. Had you known me first, perhaps we could have been good friends.

C: 别…别动我…
E: Don't... Don't touch me...

C: 太用力了…疼…
E: You're hurting me...

C: 最后这位，叫楠楠。
E: Last but not least, that's Nannan.

C: 嗯…我懂你的心意了！我们可以在「植物」的定义上大做文章啊！没问题，机械生物绝对没问题！
E: Hmm... I know where you're going with this! We can use a broad definition of plants to include mechanical creatures!

C: （呼…总算搞定了。修好一台机器可比用球棒砸碎它麻烦多了。）
E: (Phew... It's finally done. Repairing a machine is way more troublesome than smashing it with a baseball bat.)

C: 机械是植物…植物是机械…嗯？有点怪…算了……
E: Machines are plants... Plants are machines... Eh? It sounds a bit off... Forget it...

C: 关于「放生帮」…
E: About the Unshackled...

C: 天啊，我以前也不知道冒犯圣地是这么大的罪过…赔了好多钱啊，好多钱…我把自己的棺材本都赔进去了！
E: I had no idea that disrespecting a holy place was such a grave offense... The fine was exorbitant! I've used up all my retirement funds!

C: 那件事可太感谢您了，以后有什么事随时来找我们，您永远是咱们地衡司公廨的座上宾。
E: I really appreciate your help with that matter. Please feel free to drop by if you encounter any issues. You're always welcome at the Realm-Keeping Commission Chancery.

C: 不必害怕，到时候，只要躲在我身后就好。
E: Don't be scared. When the time comes, just hide behind me.

C: 家人啊，这还有什么要考虑的？不过我能理解，我会在这儿等你的！
E: What? What's there to think about? I get it. I'll be waiting for you here when you're ready!

C: 滚，老子没空跟你吹牛放屁。
E: I said scram. I have no time to talk nonsense with you.

C: …不过要是路上能拍下几段关于尾巴大爷的视频，起个「绥园惊现灵异鬼火，附身无辜狐人女孩」的标题，应该还挺抓眼球的？
E: ...I'm gonna see if I can get a video of Mr. Tail on the way back — I've already got the title: "Demonic Fire Suddenly Appears in Fyxestroll Garden! Innocent Foxian Girl Possessed!" What do you think? That'd get so many views...

C: 走吧。
E: Let's go.

C: 汝等的差事办得如何？
E: How goes your task?

C: 去调查其他铜镜。
E: Investigate other mirrors.

C: 不好意思…给您带来不好的体验了。
E: I'm sorry... for providing you with an unsatisfactory shopping experience.

C: 我带来了「心灵慰藉特调」。
E: I brought the Soulheal Special Mix.

C: 如果没有什么别的事，我就继续忙了。
E: If there's nothing else, I'd better get back to work.

C: 但你要先把「特调」喝了。
E: But you must first drink the special drink.

C: （一张满是灰尘和裂痕的唱片带，好像已经没法再播放了。）
E: (A record laden with dust and scratches — looks like it can't be played any longer.)

C: 嗯，想从事音乐相关的工作。
E: Yes, I'd like to work in something related to music.

C: 你们吵吧。
E: You know what? Keep fighting.

C: {NICKNAME}。
E: {NICKNAME}.

C: 明白——那就启动清除流程吧。
E: Understood. Begin the removal process.

C: 结合家族在梦境中对待莱斯利•迪恩的态度，让他们掺和进来或许不是什么好事——前提是他们还没有采取行动。
E: Considering the way The Family treated Lesley Dean inside the dream, getting them involved may not be a good idea... That's if they haven't already taken action.

C: （这个房间的住客因为花光了积蓄，被强制退房了。）
E: (The client in this room was forced to leave having exhausted all of their savings.)

C: 我会出现在这里，并非出自个人的意愿——何时「来」，我决定不了；何时「去」，我想自己做主。
E: It was not my personal will that I would appear in this place... Though I could not decide when I "came," I would like to make my own decision on when I "go."

C: 您会回来找我，这是不是意味着…您对我在现实中的境遇已经有所了解了？
E: The fact that you have come back to see me, does this mean... That you have come to some understanding of my situation in reality?

C: 抱歉，我听上去像是在无病呻吟。我们该去解放那些真实存在的灵魂了，{F#小姐}{M#先生}。
E: My apologies. I am complaining without just reason. It is time for us to go and liberate those souls that truly do exist.

C: 逝去的挚友从始至终都在纠缠着他。他以为只要找到了我，实现了他们儿时的梦想，往日的影子就会消散。
E: His lost friend has been haunting him this whole time. He thought that if he only found me and fulfilled their dream, then the dark shadow of their past would disappear.

C: 走吧，{NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}。一起去看看在她身上发生了什么。
E: Let's go, {F#Ms.}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}. Let's see what has happened to her.

C: …算了，没什么。你别在意。
E: ...Forget it. It's nothing. Don't worry about it.

C: 您觉得，在未来…在我消失之后，人们会以何种方式铭记莱斯利•迪恩？
E: In the future... After I disappear, how do you think people will remember the name "Lesley Dean"?

C: 船长居然这么年轻…
E: The captain's so young...

C: …垃圾桶。
E: ...Trashcan.

C: 一想到战略投资部的高管也会被扑朔迷离的星际股市套牢…你感觉内心稍微平衡了一些。
E: The idea that top execs in the Strategic Investment Department can also get caught up in the chaos of the stock market... it leaves you feeling a little more balanced inside.

C: （糟了，看来青雀是被这个皮皮西人的邪恶组织给绑架了……）
E: (This is bad. Looks like Qingque has been kidnapped by this evil Pepeshi organization...)

C: 呃…你会说话吗？
E: Um... Can you speak?

C: 「面目可憎的巨人」？多么丑陋的称呼！我不能放任你们侮辱我的挚友和波提欧兄弟。
E: "Hideous giants"? What a repulsive title! I can't let you insult my friend and Boothill here.

C: 超越自我，引爆能量！
E: Transcendence attained, energies unbound!

C: 很可惜，没能跟将军交手。
E: It is a shame that we never got that duel.

C: 嗯，三月小姐的提议不错，就这样办吧。
E: Not a bad suggestion at all, Miss March. Let's do that.

C: 难道说，我错过了？！
E: You don't mean... I missed her!?

C: 我也感同身受。
E: I can sympathize.

C: 这和你做不做工匠没有关系吧。
E: This has nothing to do with you being a craftsman.

C: 铖杰，想吃什么我请你。
E: Chengjie, what do you want? It's my treat.

C: 哎，没错，咱俩是同一个师傅。
E: Yep, that's right. We've studied under the same master.

C: 星槎，应该是所有仙舟住民最熟悉的风景了。
E: Starskiffs should be a familiar sight for all Xianzhou citizens.

C: 哎呀，我都叮嘱过让他别乱跑了。走吧，救人要紧！
E: Oh no, I told him not to run around. Let's go. We gotta save him!

C: 星槎乘风会有时，直挂云帆济星海。
E: To boldly sail where no starskiff has sailed before.

C: 我虽然忘记了师傅的模样和性格，但是他教给我的手艺、他教导我时说过的话，早就已经成为了我的小习惯，我的思考方式。
E: Despite forgetting my master's appearance and personality, the craftsmanship and words that he passed on to me have long since helped me form my habits and way of thinking.

C: 请{NICKNAME}大人一定要监督我完成！
E: Please, {NICKNAME} — please supervise me until I'm done!

C: 说实话，一开始我还在担心如今做出一百年前的星槎会不会没有什么实际意义。
E: Honestly speaking, I was worried whether it would be meaningless to make a starskiff from a century ago at first.

C: 只有你和我知道莱恩老师这么厉害还不行，我得让所有人都知道。
E: It's not enough that only you and I know how awesome Master Ryan is. The whole world should know of it.

C: （停航）
E: (Stop)

C: 这是一枚可使用在金人身上的标准零件。它们能够衔接外置玉兆与金人的主控系统，可客制化金人行动模式，便于工匠们自由测试。
E: This is a standard part that can be used on an aurumaton. It can link the master control system of an aurumaton to an external jade abacus, thus enabling the customization of the aurumaton's action model for craftsmen to perform testing.

C: 万一，我是说万一，公输师傅看出来这套系统的设计逻辑，他会不会质疑……
E: If Master Gongshu can figure out the concept behind the system, will he be suspicious...

C: *沉闷的机械低吼声*
E: *A muffled mechanical roar*

C: 铖杰师傅快去准备金人。
E: Chengjie, please prepare an aurumaton.

C: 因果殿中的魂魄无穷无尽，需要借助大衍穷观阵锚定逝者，请尽快调整阵法。
E: As there are countless souls in the Hall of Karma, the Matrix of Prescience Ultima is required to locate the deceased. Please adjust the matrix immediately.

C: 铖杰师傅好像给你准备了小礼物，去问问看吧。
E: Chengjie seems to have prepared a gift for you. You can ask him about it.

C: …哦，忘了介绍，这位就是{NICKNAME}，{F#她}{M#他}的名字你肯定听过。
E: ...Oh, I forgot to introduce {F#her}{M#him}. This is the trailblazer. You must have heard of {F#her}{M#him} before.

C: 怎么搞的，你今天怎么老是一惊一乍的？不是得了什么毛病吧？
E: Seriously, why are you so hyped up today?

C: 答应他吧！
E: Just accept!

C: 嗯…也许再过一段时间，我们可以找他聊聊。
E: Mm... Maybe one day we can talk about it with him... but let's give him some time.

C: 我和弟兄们会尽快将这群劫匪送进幽囚狱，善后工作也在进行，彦卿骁卫请勿担心。
E: We'll see to it that these criminals are sent to The Shackling Prison as soon as possible. The cleanup operation is already underway, Lieutenant Yanqing, so there's no need to worry about anything.

C: 你好，云璃小姐。
E: Glad to meet you, Miss Yunli.

C: 你发现了盲点，好奇怪啊…仙舟人会衰老吗？我在仙舟上可是很少见到老人，就算瞧见也是些异邦人。
E: Yeah, you've noticed that too. It's really weird... Do the Xianzhou people age? I rarely see any old people on the Xianzhou ship, and when I do, they're usually travelers.

C: 就像云骑，虽然能将药王秘传的乱党余孽铲除，却不能抚平这丹鼎司的人心。
E: Just like how the Cloud Knights can eliminate the remnants of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus, but are unable to calm the hearts and minds of the people within the Alchemy Commission.

C: 爷爷说过，物肖其主。我和你的剑谈过心了。它告诉我，你心事重重、闲愁万种，该出剑时迟不出剑，不出剑时也持不住剑……
E: Grandpa used to say that a sword reflects its master. I talked to your sword, and it told me that you've been distracted. You hesitate when you should strike, and you struggle to stay calm when your sword is unsheathed...

C: 你是谁？
E: Who are you?

C: 上决浮云，喝——
E: Through the clouds, it cleaves a path, Hah!

C: 这位老爷子说话还真是…出人意表。
E: This old man's words... always catch me off guard.

C: 公司派驻仙舟的商业代表之一。他虚伪地维持着公司文化自傲，但也压抑着对公司的不满与抱怨。\n斯科特耀武扬威的基础都是公司的<color=#dbc291ff>权威与福利</color>，小心不要让他<color=#dbc291ff>趁机仗势欺人</color>。
E: One of the representatives stationed on Xianzhou by the IPC. While he hypocritically remains proud of the IPC's corporate culture, he has also suppressed his dissatisfaction and resentment for the Corporation.\nThe basis of Skott's arrogance is the <color=#dbc291ff>authority and benefits</color> afforded by the IPC. Don't let him <color=#dbc291ff>take advantage of the situation and bully others</color>.

C: 彦卿怎么也在这儿？
E: Why are you here too, Yanqing?

C: 如果您准备好了，就请与在下对阵！
E: If you're ready, let's begin!

C: 下次吧。
E: Next time.

C: 那么，飞霄将军觉得如何？报告中的诸多疑点，是否得到了解释？
E: So, what do you think, General Feixiao? Have the doubts in the report been cleared up?

C: 幸好此行是我前来，若是换做{RUBY_B#有无}尘冥将军{RUBY_E#}或是{RUBY_B#爻光}戎韬将军{RUBY_E#}，这场问话或许就不会这么友善了。
E: You're lucky that I'm the one who came this time. If it were {RUBY_B#Youwu}the Patina Justice{RUBY_E#} or {RUBY_B#Yao Guang}the Seer Strategist{RUBY_E#}, this conversation might not be so friendly.

C: 好！年轻人心肠滚热，如此体贴，老朽感动得眼泪都掉下来了。
E: Your willingness to help is truly heartwarming, youngsters.

C: 天击将军是为「呼雷」而来？
E: So, you are here for Hoolay?

C: 判官折狱，会借助「业镜台」之类的复杂审讯系统。那东西会在每一次问答时，如同透镜般解读受审者身体的变化与信号，留下存证。
E: The judges will use a complicated interrogation device called the Karmic Mirror. It accurately tracks every physiological change during questioning and records it.

C: 毕竟在家靠帕姆，出外靠朋友。
E: Pom-Pom takes care of us at home, and friends take care of us on the road. That's just how it goes.

C: 世间至毒若能入药，换回一条无辜的生命，也或许能稍稍抵偿他所犯下的累累血罪了。
E: If we can use his toxin to create medicine and save an innocent life, it might help balance out some of the sins he has committed.

C: 绝不可让那头恶兽从牢笼中脱出！
E: We mustn't let that vile beast escape from its cage!

C: 若是能修好它，姐姐便有了暂时的凭寄之身。
E: If I can fix it up, my sister will have a temporary body.

C: 不知道就算了，眼下也不是交流杀手技巧的时候。
E: Well, never mind if you don't have any clues. Now is not the time to exchange assassination techniques.

C: 你们做不到的。眼下也不应该考虑他的安危。
E: No, you can do nothing for him now, and you shouldn't be focusing on his safety at the moment.

C: 我们大获全胜！
E: We kick all those bad guys' butts!

C: 想必战首不知道，击败你的那个女人…<color=#dbc291ff>「镜流」</color>。她最近回到了罗浮。
E: I'm afraid you're unaware, the woman who defeated you, <color=#dbc291ff>Jingliu</color>... She has recently returned to the Luofu.

C: 景元将军，怀炎将军，如我们所料…建木升起并非事情的结束，而是开始。
E: General Jing Yuan, General Huaiyan, it's just as we suspected... the rise of the Ambrosial Arbor was not the end of things, but the beginning.

C: 幽囚狱中
E: In the Shackling Prison

C: 你们统统被那个所谓的先知骗了，她只是派你们来送死。
E: That so-called prophet lied to all of you. She just sent you here to die.

C: 你可以用酷刑来折磨我，直到我开口；也可以…为我们双方节省些时间，战首。
E: You can torture me until I talk, or... let's save us both some time, Warhead.
Yes...

C: …这就是你一心想从我身上破解秘密的原因？你能听见我说话吗，椒丘？
E: ...Is that why you're so determined to learn my secrets? Do you hear me, Jiaoqiu?

C: 我说你到底走不走啊？
E: Are we going or not?

C: 三月七，星穹列车的美少女。
E: March 7th, young beauty of the Astral Express.

C: 你有什么弱点吗？
E: Do you have any weaknesses?

C: …没有秘密的人…只是被撕开皮肉，悲惨等死的猎物。
E: ...Those who have no secrets... are no more than prey... cut open and waiting for death...

C: 你不明白，呼雷，你根本不明白，我想要的是什么。
E: You do not understand, Hoolay. You have no idea what it is that I truly desire.

C: 所以，你为之而战的渴望是什么，呼雷？
E: So, what is it that you yearn for, Hoolay? Why is it that you fight?

C: *咆哮*
E: *roar*

C: 凭我所知的秘密，光是审讯就要花上许久许久。到最后出于种种利益交换，我一定会活下来。这一点你比我更明白，毕竟这就是你最爱玩弄的「权衡」之术嘛。
E: With the secrets I possess, the interrogation alone will take an eternity. And in the end, through various exchanges of interests, I am certain to survive. You understand this better than anyone, as it's the very art of "trade-offs" you love to wield.

C: 这场风波背后的威胁也需有人清理。
E: The threat behind all this chaos needs to be dealt with.

C: 老朽…无法断言元帅的决定。但我们所担忧的一切，元帅并非无所察觉。她已命令{RUBY_B#戎韬将军}爻光将军{RUBY_E#}日夜演算，占决大略。
E: Predicting the marshal's decision is beyond me, yet she's well aware of our concerns. She has tasked {RUBY_B#The Seer Strategist}General Yao Guang{RUBY_E#} with performing extrapolations day and night to gauge the situation comprehensively.

C: 可谓是一番难以言说、壮丽绝伦的图景☳☳。奥秘就在于那金发异邦人的棺椁……
E: The narrative they painted was vast and difficult to put into words ☳☳. It all revolves around the coffin of the blond traveler...

C: 嗯？匹诺康尼有谁不是我的臣民呢？不过我确实不认识这位……
E: Hmm? Who isn't my subject in Penacony? But I don't think I know this one...

C: 不能找猎犬家系帮忙吗？
E: Can't ask the Bloodhound Family for help?

C: 耍酒疯…这旁边就是「世界尽头」酒吧，耍酒疯的人可不少……
E: A drunken frenzy... This is the World's End bar right here, is it not? There are plenty of 'em around...

C: 我的荣幸，陛下。
E: It is my honor, Your Majesty.

C: 我叫赵铁柱。
E: My name is Luke Skybatter!

C: 不知道。朕实在想不明白，做了一辈子好人好事，怎么就会被刁民盯上呢？
E: No clue. I really don't understand: I've devoted my lifetime to doing good — why would disgruntled subjects be trying to hinder me?

C: 就这么办吧。
E: Let's do that then.

C: 你勾结叛党，意图推翻皇权。朕对你的处罚公平公正。
E: You consorted with insurgents in a plot to overthrow my imperial rule. My punishment to you was fair.

C: 可这…这根本不是什么「保险」！这就只是集体惩罚罢了！！！
E: But this... this isn't just some kind of insurance! You're just burning the forest for the trees!!!

C: 朕不能带你走，因为如果他们以后找到了我们，你就是最危险的证物。
E: I cannot take you with me, because if they find us in the future, you are the most dangerous piece of evidence.

C: 据我所知，卡尔德隆•查德威克并非长生种。而距离他最后一次与我通信已经过去了许多个年头。
E: As far as I know, Calderon Chadwick is not a long-life species. It has been decades since I was last in touch with him.

C: 情况越来越扑朔迷离了。
E: This is getting more and more puzzling.

C: 我已经很久没来过这里了，「白日梦」酒店的大堂还是和印象中一样铺张奢华。
E: It's been quite some time since I last came here. The lobby of The Reverie is still as extravagant as I remember.

C: 这种行为太冒险了…甚至可能会危害到实验人员的安全。
E: This is too risky... and might even compromise the researchers' safety.

C: 你早该明白的，你我在这场局里都只是棋子。棋子一旦落到棋盘上，剩下的就只有身不由己了。
E: You should have realized long ago that we're both merely pawns in this game of chess. Once we're placed on the chessboard, we are no longer in control of our fate.

C: 他们做了什么？
E: What have they done?

C: 之后会发生什么？
E: What will happen next?

C: 谢谢你，螺丝咕姆。也谢谢你，孩子。我感觉…好多了，也许从未这么好过……
E: Thank you, Screwllum, and you. I feel... much better, probably never better...

C: 在你眼前的不止是一台冰冷的装置，也是卡尔德隆•查德威克三分之一的生命。
E: Before you lies more than just a lifeless machine, it embodies a third of Calderon Chadwick's existence.

C: 关闭维生装置。
E: Deactivate the life-sustaining system.

C: 不知在梦境中沉浸许久以后，还留在现实中的查德威克是否安好。
E: I wonder, after immersing himself in the dreamscape for so long, is Dr. Chadwick doing well in the realm of reality?

C: 鸟也能当筑梦师？
E: Birds can be Dreamweavers too?

C: 还不好说…
E: It's hard to say...

C: 关于这张照片…
E: About this photo...

C: 时间会让过往的故事消磨褪色，被纸醉金迷掩埋…年轻人，我很高兴你还能记得他们。
E: Time will cause the colors of past tales to fade, buried by extravagance and embellishment... Young one, I'm very glad that you still remember them.

C: 桌上放着一张崭新的《逐梦客时讯》，头条处的大字印着这样一条报道。
E: A glossy new Dreamchaser Bulletin is placed on the desk, with the following header printed in huge letters.

C: 「乔伊女士，您对『白日梦酒店』的设计与艾普瑟隆的某处建筑十分相似，您对此有何解释吗？」——哇，我听着都要吐了。你这都能忍？
E: "Lady Joey, your design for the Reverie Hotel looks highly similar to a certain building in Epsilon. Do you have an explanation for this?" Sheesh, just listening to this tripe makes me want to puke. You can tolerate this?

C: 孩子，你是在哪学到的筑梦技巧？
E: Child, where did you learn these dreamweaving skills?

C: 当然是一样………
E: In the same way, obviously...

C: 吾师，会算账不意味着要还账…别坑我啊！
E: Teacher, being good at the ledger isn't the same as paying for stuff... Please don't tease me!

C: 记得别让卫兵看见箱子，不然可能会有麻烦——那就不关我的事了。
E: Don't let the guards see the crate, or there'll be trouble — I won't be liable for any of that.

C: 给你们的，还不快道谢？
E: This is for you. How about a thank-you?

C: 我一路跟踪那帮卫兵，发现他们居然把吾师押去了<color=#dbc291ff>云石天宫的上层</color>！那里人多眼杂，我没法靠得太近，只好回来找你商量对策。
E: I tailed the guards and found that they had detained Master on the <color=#dbc291ff>upper levels of Marmoreal Palace</color>! There were a lot of people there, and I couldn't get close, so I hurried back to discuss a plan with you.

C: 当然，总有人觉得自己不会陷入困境。只是…谁又能保证奥赫玛的城墙能永远挺立呢？
E: Of course, there are always people who think they'll never find themselves in hardship. Except... who can ensure that Okhema's city walls will stand tall forever?

C: 他会面临怎样的命运？
E: What fate is in store for him?

C: 其实我很好奇，灰发的贵客对此会有何见解？作为和黄金裔并肩作战过的英雄，你的想法至关重要。
E: I am curious: How does our gray-haired guest view this situation? As the hero who fought alongside the Chrysos Heirs, your thoughts may be of crucial import.

C: 一桩不当判决对社会风气造成的破坏，十个恶人都比不上。公民们，三思啊，不能开这个坏头。
E: The damage of a single wrongful ruling on the moral fabric of society is greater than that of ten wrongdoers. Citizens, think carefully — we cannot set such a bad precedent.

C: 你疑似有些小题大做了。
E: You're trying to make a mountain out of a molehill.

C: 各位先前的讨论，风儿已顺着金丝为我转述。在这个非常时刻，比起争辩衣服上的漏洞是对是错，或许我们更应该考虑怎么去缝补它。
E: Everyone's earlier discussions have been conveyed to me through golden threads riding the winds. At such a critical time, instead of arguing over whether the holes in a garment are right or wrong, perhaps we should consider how we should mend them instead.

C: 我们的贵客给出了不错的思路。顺着{F#她}{M#他}的话头，我来为各位公民提出一个折衷方案——
E: Our honored guest has offered some good ideas. Following {F#her}{M#his} thoughts, I would like to offer a compromise to the citizens...

C: 但这未必不是件好事。群众有时会被不那么明智的声音裹挟，而你不会成为其中一员。
E: But it may not be a bad thing. The crowd may be swayed by less wise voices, but you will not be one of them.

C: 英雄，请务必小心。
E: Hero, please be careful.

C: 是啊，我肯定得跑——要是死在战场上，可就没机会去<color=#dbc291ff>阿卡迪亚</color>了。
E: That's right, I'd have to run. If I die on the battlefield, I'll never get my chance to go to <color=#dbc291ff>Arcadia</color>.

C: 喂，这是咱们奥赫玛的客人，别这么无礼。
E: Hey! This is a guest of Okhema. Don't be so rude.

C: 没有，估计是作者开新坑去了。现在大伙更爱看黄金裔英雄跟黑潮怪物对抗的剧情，这种乌托邦题材已经过时啦。
E: It does not. I imagine writer have moved on to other interests. Nowadays, people want to read about the heroes of Chrysos Heir doing battle with the monsters of the black tide. This kind of utopian storylines has grown old now.

C: 帕里斯从衣物中拿出了一枚纺锤，上面缠绕着几条金色的丝线，在黎明之下熠熠生辉。
E: Paris produces a spindle from his robes, around which a number of golden threads glisten in the dawn light.

C: 别哭，坏人会笑。
E: Don't cry. Your enemies will laugh.

C: 我为什么要哭…而且我也没有制造泪水的能力。
E: Why would I cry...? I'm not even capable of producing tears.

C: 呵呵，朝夕相伴这么久了，而且明眼人都能看出我的心意。
E: Hah, we have spent many days and nights together now. Anyone with sense can see how I feel for her.

C: 现在，我要向她表明我的心意与愿望。请你为我见证，为我祝福，给我力量…这是我最后拜托你的一个忙了。
E: Now, I will confess my love and wishes to her. Please bear witness and give me your blessings and strength... This is the last favor I will ask of you.

C: 可是…可是除了她，还有谁愿意帮我抛下这可怕的责任与义务，相信我所说的故事，和我一起去寻找阿卡迪亚呢？
E: But... But if I don't have her, who else will help me escape my terrifying duties and responsibilities? Who will believe my stories and go in search of Arcadia with me?

C: 现在，那金丝已断了，你手中再也不会有首饰、厨具和针线，只剩长弓与利箭。
E: Now, the thread is cut. Your hands will never again hold silver trinkets, kitchenware, or tapestries. All that remains are arrows and weaponry.

C: 开场的记忆
E: Opening Memory

C: 太懈怠了，奥赫玛人！刻法勒没教你们准时劳作的美德吗？
E: You Okhemans are so slothful! Has Kephale never taught you the virtue of punctuality in your labors?

C: 故事的画风都变了。
E: The tone of the story has shifted.

C: 来到奥赫玛后，帕西提亚女士很快融入了进来。她为被命运选中的人们提供帮助，也会例行给予我们一些谕示，就像今天这样。
E: After arriving at Okhema, Lady Pasithea quickly integrated herself. She offers her help to those chosen by destiny and routinely bestows her prophecies on us, just like today.

C: 「死亡！我将回归塞纳托斯的怀抱！」得赶快找到她！
E: "Death! I will return to the embrace of Thanatos!" We have to find her quickly!

C: 好险！开拓者啊开拓者，差点就忘了你肩负的重要使命？你的大脑以超负荷的状态高速思考，怎样才能阻止她在你面前跳下去。
E: That was a close one! Trailblazer, oh Trailblazer, have you forgotten the important mission you carry? Your brain runs at full capacity, thinking of ways to stop her from jumping down.

C: 肚子因饥饿而发出声响，迅速打断了亵渎的思绪。
E: A growl escapes her hungry stomach, swiftly cutting off the blasphemous thoughts.

C: 欧洛尼斯的神谕已经降下，出自泰坦之语，不可曲解，不可动摇的命运。我们终会战胜死亡和纷争，众神的目光将照耀我们荣耀的前路！
E: Oronyx's prophecy has been bestowed. They are the words of the Titans. They form untwistable and unwavering destiny. We will eventually triumph over death and strife. May the gods' gazes shine radiant over our glorious advance!

C: 久违地，你感受到饥饿。你还记得离开时，母亲对你说的最后一句话，她努力挤出笑容，只要稍微松动嘴角，眼泪就会沿着皱纹流下。
E: After a long time, you feel famished, a sensation you have not felt for a long time. You still remember the last words your mother said to you when you left her. She tried her hardest to force a smile — as soon as the corners of her lips drooped, her tears would flow down her wrinkled crevices.

C: 时间流转，黑潮与死亡也在命运揭示的时刻到来——
E: As time marches on, the black tide and death both arrive at the moment revealed by fate...

C: 没有人能逃出命运。
E: No one can escape destiny.

C: 来吧，找到了，声音的方向！
E: Come on, I found the direction of the sound!

C: 一二三四五，丹恒打虚卒。
E: One, two, three, four, five... Dan Heng and Voidranger jive.

C: 既然如此，我们就交换着看吧。不过，你可不准在书上乱涂乱画哦。
E: In that case, let's take turns reading it. But hey, no doodling or scribbling in the book, alright?

C: 那是段年少轻狂的岁月。若要我再选一次，大抵还会走上相同的路——就像你在书中对「开拓」的自述那样。
E: Those were the reckless days of my youth. If I had to choose again, I'd probably walk the same path, same as your account of the Trailblaze in your book.

C: 「记忆」是种奇妙的东西。有时，我甚至觉得它是{RUBY_B#浮黎}流光天君{RUBY_E#}的恶作剧。
E: "Memory" is quite a peculiar thing. Sometimes, I even think it's a prank by the {RUBY_B#Fuli}Glowswather{RUBY_E#}.

C: 你想起钟表小子、哈努兄弟、音符小姐…它们也曾是活生生的传奇。倘若《如我所书》以同样戏谑的形式被传唱，是你想要的结果吗？
E: You remember Clockie, Brother Hanu, Miss Note... they were all living legends once. If As I've Written gets passed down in the same mocking way, is that really what you want?

C: 说不定有朝一日，我们就能走进那个忆质世界，跟书里的英雄面对面交谈了。
E: Maybe we'll be able to step into that memoria world one day and have face-to-face conversations with the heroes from that book.

C: 你说，这白厄真君和景元将军，孰强孰弱啊？
E: Who do you think is stronger, Lord Phainon or General Jing Yuan?

C: 啊，对啦……
E: Oh, that's right...

C: 也许，是时候履行诺言了。
E: Perhaps it's time to fulfill my promise.

C: （然后，是这段旅途的意义……）
E: (And now, for the meaning of this journey...)

C: 嗯…嗯…好的好的，哇！还蛮顺口的！
E: Mmm... Mmm... sure, sure. Wow! That rolls off the tongue!

C: 机器人出事了？
E: Something happened with the robots?

C: {NICKNAME}，我说的没错吧？重新启动就完事了，多简单！
E: See, {NICKNAME}, I'm right! They just needed rebooting. Easy!

C: 你怎么这么困？
E: Why are you so sleepy?

C: 说出来你别笑…就是我刚刚坐着的…「马桶」。
E: Well, just don't laugh... It's... the toilet seat I was sitting on.

C: 哎，我也拿不准……
E: *sigh* I can't really decide...

C: 哦？你还真是自信满满。
E: Oh? You're pretty confident.

C: 你怎么不自己去？
E: Why don't you do it yourself?

C: …那我这个做朋友的说不定也会受到影响，仕途受挫！
E: ...Then I might also be affected, and my career will be ruined!

C: 这是什么？华劳斯？旁边还围着一群小孩…这不是他的小孩吧？
E: What's this? Is that Wallace? He's surrounded by a bunch of kids... These are not his children, are they?

C: 他是莱姆士汀家的公子。
E: He's a Limestein.

C: 一个空空如也的垃圾桶，似乎已被人光顾过。垃圾消失在垃圾桶里，就像水滴消失在水塘中。
E: An empty trashcan. Looks like it's been cleared out. Trash disappearing into a trashcan like droplets of water disappearing into a pond.

C: {M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}找到其它纸团了吗？快给阿丽娜看看吧。
E: So, you've found all the other paper balls? Please show them to me.

C: 按照巴列维的指示果然找到了古代遗物，他甚至没有来过这里，他是怎么知道的呢？
E: You found the ancient relics that Balaway was looking for. Without having been here, how did he know where to look?

C: 秘密任务就是偷人家的东西？
E: So the secret mission is to steal from others?

C: 我还是没太明白…
E: I still don't quite get it...

C: 唔，之前「地火」的大人遇到这种情况，总是要纠结很久——也就是说，要是我们能把这两份物资都快速送达，那鼹鼠党就完成了「地火」做不到的事情…对吧？
E: Well, the grown-ups from Wildfire always waste a lot of time deciding which one to deliver first — so if we can make these two deliveries quickly, it would mean The Moles can achieve what Wildfire can't... right?

C: 真的吗？那就全靠你了，歌蒂阿姨！
E: Really? Thank you, Ms. Gertie!

C: 如果没有凭证，你打算怎么办？
E: What were you going to do if I didn't have a pass?

C: 咳…我准备好了！你们先上吧，我、我会尽全力协助你们的！
E: Ahem... I'm ready! You guys go first. I—I'll support you from behind!

C: 我没见过他的妻子和孩子，不过…我觉得她们不会在意什么战功勋章。
E: I haven't met his wife or child, but... I think they won't care about whether or not he has a medal.

C: 可那几个尸位素餐的官僚，一直在想方设法刁难我！
E: But those pencil-pushing bureaucrats are always trying to make trouble for me!

C: 我不想再答题了！
E: I don't want to answer any more questions!

C: 请您慢走。
E: So long.

C: 咳，抱歉，请允许我回避您的这次提问。请问您还有别的事吗？
E: Ahem, sorry. Please allow me to sidestep this question of yours. Is there anything else I can do for you?

C: 咳，抱歉，请允许我回避您的这次提问。请问您还有别的事吗？
E: Ahem, sorry. Please allow me to sidestep this question of yours. Is there anything else I can do for you?

C: 嗯…很遗憾，回答错误。
E: Hmm... I'm afraid that's incorrect.

C: 嗯…很遗憾，回答错误。
E: Hmm... I'm afraid that's incorrect.

C: 打开有怪物的那扇。
E: Open the one with monsters.

C: 咦？您的意思是……
E: Hmm? Do you mean...

C: 没错，灵魂…小女子觉得自己的临摹已经非常完美了，可她一颦一笑之中蕴含的气场，小女子却怎么也学不来……
E: Yeah, soul... my mimicry's already pretty much perfect, but the presence and gravitas contained in all those micro-expressions is something I haven't been able to master...

C: 小女子意识到，任何人只要勤加练习，都能临摹她的肉体…但想要真正地成为塔米拉小姐，还需临摹她的灵魂。
E: I know that anyone can just copy her body as long as you practice hard enough... but I'll need to copy her soul if I want to truly become Tamila.

C: 你可以帮帮我吗？
E: Can you help me?

C: 你能这么说，小女子太开心了。谢谢你~
E: Ah, that makes me so happy! Thank you~

C: 多亏了你~还挺顺利的~小女子已经能看到自己在舞台上光彩夺目的身影了…总有一天，小女子也能像她那样……
E: Thanks to you~ everything's going great. I can already see my dazzling figure on stage... One day, I'm going to be just like her...

C: 嗯…这里和想象中的氛围不太一样啊。
E: Hmm... the ambiance here is not what I expected.

C: 哈哈，既然你这么说，那我可不能再谢绝你的好意了。
E: Hahaha. I won't refuse your help then.

C: 哎…你别添乱！我们这是志愿者团体——专门做义工、做好事的！
E: Hey, don't make it worse! We are a volunteer group — We volunteer and do good deeds!

C: 总之，我们是个造福社会的公益组织，而我老歌德正是行政区分社的负责人。我很欣赏你的热情和能力——有兴趣加入我们吗？
E: Anyways, we're a charitable organization that aims to make the world a better place, and I'm the manager for the Administrative District branch. I like your passion and capacity. Would you be interested in joining us?

C: 两小时过去……
E: Two hours later.

C: 看吧，年轻人。这是我的家人，这位是我的妻子…那时候，真是我人生中最幸福的时候啊。
E: Look, kid. This is my family, and this is my wife... That was the happiest period of my life.

C: 她可是…她可是带领我们击败了那些从天上来的怪物们啊！怪物们都没办法伤到她…她怎么会死呢？
E: She... She led us to victory against the monsters from the sky! Even the monsters couldn't hurt her... How could she die?

C: 可怜的铁卫不再出声。你将她上身扶起，轻柔地落在地面上，又将那柄久经沙场的钢枪放入她的怀中，试图以符合战士身份的、体面的葬礼为她送行。
E: The poor guard stops making a sound. You gently place his body into a sitting position and put his battle-hardened spear into his arms, trying to give him a respectable funeral worthy of a warrior.

C: （这是捏造的故事？越来越神秘了……）
E: (So it's a made-up story? This keeps getting more and more mysterious...)

C: 你反正都要扔掉，我拿着怎么了？别盯着我了，马上就走。
E: You were going to throw it away anyway, what's wrong with me taking it? Don't stare at me like that, I'm leaving right away.

C: 落难人希望你能作为受苦之人劝劝「星期六」不要回到文明社会。来，写点什么吧。
E: The castaway hopes that you, as someone who suffers inside civilized society, can convince Saturday not to return to it. It's time to write something.

C: …这也许是「丰饶」命途的体现。至少在当下，你走在「丰饶」的命途上。下次去模拟宇宙的时候，试试「丰饶」开局如何？
E: ...Perhaps this is a manifestation of the Path of Abundance. At least, for now, you are ambling on the Path of Abundance. How about trying Abundance at the start of your next Simulated Universe?

C: 杜老板面带笑意的目光将你从恐惧的深渊中拉了回来，但你分不清这究竟是往回拉，还是往前推。终于，你鼓起勇气，凝望着「烈焰浓茶」，直面着自己内心的恐惧，捏着鼻子将它一饮而尽！
E: Boss Du's smiling gaze snaps you back from the fearsome abyss, although you are not sure if he's pulling you away from it or pushing you towards it. In the end, you muster up your courage, stare directly at the Flaming Potent Tea and your inner fears, pinch your nose, and just like that, drink it up!

C: 哦对，我怎么把你给忘了。正巧你在呢！怎么样，{NICKNAME}，来当我的「视茶员」如何？该有的报酬定不会短了你的。
E: Oh, how could I possibly forget you, {NICKNAME}! Why don't you try my new products and give me your thoughts? You'll receive compensation for your help, of course.

C: 这么说来，这方子有问题，得再调整下配方了。哦对了，这报酬答应你的，你收好，回头等我捣鼓出新品了再喊你。
E: In any case, something is wrong with this recipe, and I'll have to adjust it. Oh, here's the promised compensation. I'll give you a call once I've come up with something new.

C: 名为水漫琴的乐器。个别信奉法吉娜的城邦会把它抬上战场，作为扰乱敌人军心的手段使用。
E: An instrument called a water harp. Some cities that worship Phagousa transport it onto the battlefield as a method of disrupting enemy troop momentum.

C: 腾腾热气让你有些睁不开眼。温暖的事物总是模糊不清——你发出感慨。
E: You have to squint your eyes from the steaming heat. Events exuding warmth are always hazy — you let out a sigh.

C: 瑟希斯的赐福
E: The Benediction of Cerces

C: 公司将秉持「存护」之道，为该星体提供必要的保障和引导。博识学会已就此向两位天才发出合作邀请，其中，黑塔女士尚未给出肯定答复。
E: The IPC will uphold the principles of the Preservation and provide essential protection and guidance for this planet. The Intelligentsia Guild has invited both geniuses to collaborate on this endeavor. However, Madam Herta has yet to give a definitive response.

C: 我呼唤最远古的黑夜，祈求它带来最恒远的光明。
E: I call on the dark night of the very first age, and pray that it brings the most timeless light.

C: 奥赫玛的市集
E: Okhema Market

C: 偶有失落城邦的遗物现于市集，尽管多是最后的城邦自我慰藉的幻想。
E: The market occasionally sees relics from the lost cities, but they mostly turn out to be from the self-soothing fantasies of the last city.

C: 琢石区
E: Chisel Zone

C: 温柔地缠绕，令你火光熊熊，而后缓缓熄灭。」
E: Gently entwining, I stoke your fire to brilliance, then let it slowly die down."

C: 哦？您莫非也……
E: Oh? Are you also...

C: 这名字并非源于父母。我曾徒手搏杀过一头雄狮，与「搦狮之王」歌耳戈立下了相似的伟业。
E: It was not my parents who named me this. I once killed a lion with my bare hands, achieving a feat similar to that of Gorgo: "Conqueror of Lions."

C: …朕并非此意。刚才，若你在我手下坚持十合未倒，那么这顶桂冠与你正登对。
E: ...You misunderstand me. Just now, if you had managed to last ten rounds against me, the crown would be rightfully yours.

C: 预言已经不容置喙地指明，您的孩子迈德漠斯，将夺走您的性命、断绝尼卡多利的永世长存，甚至悬锋王朝，都将在他手中终结……
E: The prophecy has already stated without question that your child, Mydeimos, will take your life, bring an end to Nikador's eternal existence, and even the dynasty of Kremnos will fall at his hands...

C: 金色的光辉从神坛底部的根系间流淌而出，像是墨涅塔的丝线编织出的记忆之网。
E: Golden light flows out from the roots beneath the altar, like threads spun by Mnestia, weaving a web of memories.

C: 没有华丽的装饰，没有繁复的图案，斑驳的岩刻壁画上只有一个简单剪影立于光芒之中。
E: No lavish decorations, no intricate patterns — the weathered rock mural bears only a plain silhouette bathed in light.

C: 妈妈！你看这个！我失败了好多次，终于成功啦！
E: Momma! Look at this! It kept going wrong but I finally made one that works!

C: …库娜涅，转头看看吧……
E: Cynane... behind you...

C: 熄灭圣烛。
E: Extinguish the holy candle.

C: 你没逝吧？
E: You're not dead, are you?

C: 大地兽肉排！
E: Dromas steak!

C: 用小海豹水上对战？
E: Water fight with baby seals?

C: 别，别拿我跟那些搞古玩的怪人混为一谈。
E: No, no, don't lump me in with those antique weirdos.

C: …是啊。说的夸张些，哪怕昏光庭院建在重渊，风堇小姐也会把那片黑漆漆的地方照亮吧。
E: ...That's true. To put it bluntly, even if the Twilight Courtyard were built in the Abyss, Miss Hyacine would still light up that dark place.

C: 怎么可能呢，这世上没人跑得过翻飞之币。
E: No way. No one in this world can outrun the Coin of Whimsy.

C: 止步吧卑劣残缺的胎生种，回去大地那溺爱你们的摇床。
E: Cease your advance, vile and flawed mammals, back to the land's pampering cradle.

C: 闪烁着星光的纹路带，仿佛夜空中的绸缎。它的源头似乎是个谜。
E: A band of shimmering starlit patterns, like silk draped across the night sky. Its origin remains a mystery.

C: 大门上方的光源发出柔和的蓝光，在黎明与黄昏的交替中，它见证了翁法罗斯的兴衰。而你的出现，只是它漫长凝视中的一个瞬间。
E: The light above the gate emits a soft blue glow, witnessing the rise and fall of Amphoreus between dawn and dusk. Your presence is but a fleeting moment in its long, unwavering gaze.

C: 那鱼呢？
E: Then where are these fish now?

C: 你努力凑近窗户，试图窥视屋内的陈设，却只能看见一团温馨的光。
E: You lean in close to the window, hoping to peek inside, but all you see is a soft, cozy glow.

C: 毫无疑问，你曾在故事里见过这个庞大的木制道具，瞧它那高高昂起的头颅，以及宽广到足以装进好几个人的空洞腹部——所有特征都与你的认知相符。
E: No doubt you recognize this massive wooden prop from stories — its proud head held high, with a belly so big it could fit several people. Every detail matches what you know.

C: 要在平时，你保准会冒出个钻进去看看的念头——很可惜，现在的你，是只巨大的扎格列斯之手。
E: Normally, you'd probably want to crawl inside and take a look, but unfortunately, you're the giant Hand of Zagreus right now.

C: …伟大的翻飞之手？
E: ...The mighty Whimsy Hand?

C: 眼前飞来飞去的坛递让你不由陷入沉思——这玩意算是醉驾吗？超速了吗？
E: You watch these floating amphorae wobble through the air and wonder... does that count as flying under the influence? Are they speeding?

C: 你觉得，该打多少分？
E: What score do you think that deserves?

C: 斯缇科西亚的高塔，破损的墙垣，以及双月泄下的如注月华，以及盛开的「安提灵」。
E: The tower of Styxia, the crumbling walls, the twin moons pouring down their radiant light, and the blooming Antila.

C: 我将把冥界变作温柔的归处。
E: I shall turn the nether realm into a gentle place of rest.

C: 回想一下我们不长不短的照面，你对我始终警惕，我们甚至没有为一场正式的「告别」留出足够的空间，不是吗？
E: Thinking back to our brief yet lingering encounters, how wary you were of me. We never even had the time for a proper "farewell," did we?

C: 呵呵，正是因为我们是「大地」的子民，才要仰望星空。
E: Heh, it's precisely because we're children of Earth that we must gaze up at the stars.

C: 免礼免礼，无敌什么都好，就是太寂寞…要不你把那爱下棋的小凯撒找来？
E: No need for formalities. Being invincible is fine, but it is too lonely... Why not bring that chess-loving little Imperator here?

C: 可惜虫腿扫不动喀，不然解明真理的机会就轮不着你啦！
E: Too bad my nymph legs can't sweep a thing now, crrk. Otherwise you wouldn't even have gotten a chance to uncover the truth!

C: 世界的真理…
E: The truth of this world...

C: 还没等你反应过来，女孩就跨出了镜子。
E: Before you can react, the girl steps out of the mirror.

C: 好呀，你问吧。
E: Sure, ask away.

C: 离开。
E: Leave.

C: ——不过，我猜你们手头上还有更要紧的事，对吧？那就祝你们万事顺利。
E: ...But I guess you have more pressing matters to attend to, yes? Then I wish you all the best.

C: 还是算了。
E: Forget it.

C: 惩奸除恶，义不容辞。
E: I'll do my best to punish and eliminate the evil.

C: 行。既然你都答应了，我也就不客气了。
E: Alright. You've agreed. Let's ramp things up.

C: 再给我讲讲这次行动计划。
E: Tell me more about this current mission.

C: 您发现了？太好了！我正想告诉您，我说话不利索的毛病已经彻底改正了！
E: You can tell? That's great! I'm just about to tell you how my stuttering has been completely fixed!

C: 佩佩朝你笑了笑，尾巴摇动得比之前还要欢快。
E: Peppy smiles at you, its tail wagging more profusely than before with happiness.

C: 谢谢你们救了我！
E: Thank you for saving me!

C: （我知道那家伙在哪儿了，就躲在禁闭舱段里！）
E: (I know where it is — it's hiding in the seclusion zone!)

C: （这套门禁系统还能不能好了。）
E: (Is this access control system going to be any good?)

C: 噢，你用终端和她联系。可是使用记录里压根没有你！
E: Oh, you used the terminal to communicate with her. But the communication history clearly does not reflect it!

C: 汪汪！汪！
E: Woof woof! Woof!

C: 佩佩难以置信地望着你。
E: Peppy stares at you in bewilderment.

C: 他和白厄谁厉害？
E: Who's stronger, him or Phainon?

C: 怎么会？一起留下回忆，不是会有更多话题吗？
E: That doesn't make sense! The more memories we make together, the more there is to talk about.

C: 这样呀，但人家也很喜欢这样的故事——想想看，那些我们都喜欢的美好结局，不总是故事里的大家仍在继续生活，「还没有迎来结局」吗？
E: I see... but I actually really enjoy stories like this — just think about it. Those perfect endings we all love? Aren't they always the ones where the story keeps going, where everyone's still living, "the ending yet to come"?

C: 唔…{NICKNAME}，你这次在梦里怎么不是毛茸茸的了…
E: Hmm... {NICKNAME}, why aren't you all fluffy in my dream this time...

C: 真希望关于他们的一切，永远不会迎来终点呀。
E: I really hope everything about them never reaches an end.

C: 货运星槎缓缓驶入了码头。
E: The freight starskiff slowly moves into the jetty.

C: （要不是为了这点儿小费……）
E: (If not for these tips...)

C: 稍等，我还有点事。
E: Just a moment, I have something else to do.

C: 稍等一下，我好像有一点儿灵感，让我静一静。
E: Wait a minute. I have a little inspiration. Leave me alone.

C: 太卜大人吩咐我在此维护穷观阵，工作的其中一项便是例行占算。
E: The master diviner assigned me here to maintain the Matrix of Prescience, one task of which is routine divinations.

C: 喂，这玩笑可开的不太妙啊。那绘星在此祝你每天都有好心情吧。
E: Hey, that's not funny. Well then, wishing you a good mood every day!

C: 那就开始吧。
E: Then let's start.

C: …我没有很激动。
E: ...Ahem. I'm not that excited.

C: …我总得调整一下心态嘛。
E: ...Well, I got to shake up my mood, right?

C: 来吧。
E: Come on then.

C: 除了我。
E: Except me.

C: 废话不多说，准备接受我的审判吧。
E: Alright, let's cut the chatter. Prepare yourself for judgment!

C: 你想见见我们老大吗？
E: Would you like to meet our boss?

C: 有成为系列作品的打算，目前只有一作。
E: There are plans to make it a series. We have only one game so far.

C: 滴、答…滴、答…滴、答……
E: Tick, tock... Tick, tock... Tick, tock...

C: 不知道能不能帮上你的忙，游客！
E: I wonder if this will help you, guest!

C: 嗯…有位织者说要当上雅努斯的祭司。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>悬崖边的护栏上</color>练习穿墙术，可以去检验下它的成果。
E: Hmm... There is a weaver who wishes to become Janus' priest. The golden thread brings tidings: They now stand <color=#dbc291ff>on the railing at the edge of the cliff</color>, honing their skill in wall penetration. Feel free to go and test the fruits of their practice.

C: 「蝴蝶知道一切」。
E: "Butterflies know everything."

C: 多谢你了，贵客。但曾有贤者说过这样一句话：「人无法同时阅读两本书。」
E: Thank you, my esteemed guest. But a wise man once said, "No man can read two books at the same time."

C: 先回答我一个问题吧，年轻人：在纷争的剑锋之下，总共有多少位勇士追随着持矛君王？
E: Answer this one question then, youngling: Under the blades of Strife, how many warriors followed the lance-wielding king in total?

C: <unbreak>15</unbreak>位。
E: Fifteen warriors.

C: 刻法勒在上，我这把老骨头啊…祭司说我八百八十四岁必有一劫，莫非就是今天？
E: Kephale above. I am just an old man... The priest said that I would go through an ordeal when I was <unbreak>884</unbreak> years old... Could this be the day?

C: 「投枪扎入大地，泥土啜饮奠品」，吟游诗人将战士们的厮杀编入韵文，可惜盛况早已不再，歌谣现下也鲜有听到了。
E: "Cast your spear into the earth and feed the soil its libations." Bards and poets compiled songs of the warriors' battles to the death. Unfortunately, this prosperity has long since passed, and these songs are rarely heard anymore.

C: 呃啊，不公平，一定是欧洛尼斯在暗中相助！
E: Aghh, that's not fair... Oronyx must have been helping you from the shadows!

C: 嘁…满嘴跑马车的家伙，竟然还有两下子！
E: Whew... You sure got some skills for someone who talks tough!

C: 又在打什么坏主意？
E: What're you up to this time?

C: 你好像有点急了…
E: You sound agitated...

C: 大人的事小孩少管。
E: This is grown-up stuff, kid. Run along.

C: 哎呀哎呀，看着你在战斗中的曼妙身姿，也是如蝴蝶起舞一般呐！
E: My my, your graceful fighting style — it's like watching a butterfly dance!

C: 比方说…伙计，你有没有想过，一个「死人的世界」究竟意味着什么？
E: Think about it, have you ever pondered what a "world of the dead" actually means?

C: 从一开始就是你的诡计。
E: You planned this whole thing from the start.

C: 老巴特鲁斯的朋友很少。但您是它极重要的朋友。愿{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}翻飞之币{RUBY_E#}祝福你们在再创世之后相见。桀桀。
E: Old Bartholos has few friends, but you are most precious to it. May the {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Coin of Whimsy{RUBY_E#} ensure you meet again after Era Nova. Hehe.
Hee...

C: 哎呀哎呀，相隔了上千年，还是和您交手来得带劲啊！
E: Well, well, even after a thousand years, nothing gets my blood pumping like a good brawl with you!

C: 别想借机从我这里偷东西。
E: Don't even think about trying to steal from me.

C: ——才怪。被困在这些讨厌的柱子中间，我都快变成安提基色拉人的动力核心了！
E: ...Just kidding! Being stuck between these awful pillars, I feel like I'm about to become the Antikytherans' energy core myself!

C: 你面前的大家伙纹丝不动。仔细一看，大家伙的旁边压着一张字条……
E: The huge entity in front of you won't budge. Upon closer inspection, you see a note pinned next to it...

C: 诶，别这么说嘛！不管你嘴上怎么说，你的脚都已经走到这一步了不是？
E: Don't say that! You're here already, aren't you?

C: 我有一个大胆的假设——一旦它成立，或许世界上所有的未解之谜就全都说的通了！
E: I have a bold hypothesis that, if proven true, can explain just about every unsolved mystery in this world!

C: 恩维尔同学谬赞了。只是安提基色拉人天然擅长记住各种零碎的知识，所以能帮助奥赫玛做一些力所能及的工作。
E: Avilius, you flatter me. Antikytherans are naturally good at remembering bits and pieces of knowledge, so I simply assist Okhema in ways I can.

C: 「吕枯耳戈斯」。如果你不喜欢这个名字，我对此表示抱歉。
E: "Lycurgus." If that name displeases you, I apologize.

C: 门径的禁制居然没拦住你，并且…将庞杂的溯寻推演称作玩乐，或许你就是我要找的人。
E: The containment mechanism didn't stop you, and you even call the intricate extrapolating process a game... Perhaps you're exactly the person I've been looking for.

C: 不出所料，冥界…塞纳托斯的领域。再合理不过的解答。
E: As expected... The nether realm — Thanatos' domain. A perfectly reasonable answer.

C: 我想，我们还需要一次推演，这会是最后一次了。
E: I think we need one final extrapolation. This will be the last attempt.

C: 想必这样的谜题也难不倒你吧，加油，哗啦啦——
E: I bet this puzzle won't stump you. Go for it! *whoosh*

C: 皎洁的月光从窗外射入，如奶油花般涂抹在地上。
E: The bright moonlight shines through the window, spreading across the floor like creamy frosting.

C: 啊…你…你是……？
E: Ah... you... you're...?

C: 时间紧迫，仪式并不完全，破碎的记忆像潮汐，要把沙滩上的一切抹去…好在最后，它也将那个答案送到了我的面前。
E: The ritual was rushed, incomplete. My memories are breaking apart, like waves washing over the shore, erasing everything in their path... But in the end, at least it gave me the answer.

C: 希望…我们能在西风的尽头…在那个阳光明媚、没有伤痛的世界重新相识。
